_notes: 2026-06-23 (guide-02-chargen-authoritative, Opus loop): GUIDES authoritative quality pass on Guide_02 Character Creation (the first guide every new player reads). Cross-checked every quantified claim against HEAD: 9 species attribute ranges + Move + special abilities (data/species/*.yaml), 76 skills (data/skills.yaml), 9 chargen templates with full attribute+skill stats (chargen_templates.yaml), 18D/54-pip attr + 7D/21-pip skill budgets + exact-spend/under-spend rules (engine/creation.py _validate), +2D-per-skill cap (MAX_SKILL_BONUS_PIPS), 1 FP / 5 CP / 1000 cr starting defaults (Character) + 500-cr blaster anchor (weapons.yaml), wizard step ladder + nav verbs (back/free/guided/undo/sheet/quit) + first-char-mandatory/alt-next-skip tutorial-chain policy (creation_wizard.py), and the Force unlock path (village_choice._seed_force_attributes seeds 1D control/sense/alter, FP unchanged — derived state, no chargen toggle). Command refs verified post-syntax-rework: +char, +teach, bare train, chargen-mode info. FIXES: (1) §1 Overview removed the stale 'optionally declare Force sensitivity' step (STEP_FORCE retired in PG.3.gates.b; contradicted §6 + HEAD) and rewrote the flow to the real step order. (2) §2 tightened the vague Duros (+1D Astrogation) and Sullustan (+1D Astrogation/Search, +1D Perception) abilities to their species-YAML mechanics. Version 1.0 -> 1.1. Guard: tests/test_guide_02_chargen_rework.py +10 cross-check tests (32 total green) pinning the documented numbers to live engine+data. Docs+test only; engine+data READ-ONLY; era-clean; no schema; no faucet/sink.

2026-06-21 (space-breakit-fixes, Sonnet drop-implementer): 7 confirmed space-subsystem defects from adversarial break-it sweep fixed. (1) _uninstall_mod NameError: template.mod_slots → t.mod_slots (walrus binding was correct, f-string name was wrong) in parser/space_commands.py. (2) sell cargo 0 ZeroDivisionError: quantity < 1 guard added to _handle_sell_cargo in parser/builtin_commands.py. (3) docking_fee TOCTOU: replaced min(stale_cache,fee)+unguarded debit with single adjust_credits(-fee,'docking_fee',allow_negative=False) + None-abort (player still lands on overdraw refused) in parser/space_commands.py. (4) Telnet space_choices* repr leak: space_choices/space_choices_dismiss/space_choices_countdown added to silent-pass set in server/session.py. (5) buy cargo supply cap bypass: when planet is falsy (never-launched ship), block with 'No active cargo market at this location' before SUPPLY_POOL.available() in parser/space_commands.py. (6) sell cargo demand pool bypass: same planet=None block added before get_planet_price() in parser/builtin_commands.py. (7) space_fine TOCTOU: same allow_negative=False pattern as #3 in parser/space_commands.py. New tests/test_space_breakit_fixes_2026_06_21.py (17 tests: 9 behavioral + 8 static). No schema; era-clean; all credit movement through adjust_credits funnel.

2026-06-21 (command-telemetry, Opus loop): T3.19 telemetry breadth — instrumented the command dispatcher, the single seam that sees the whole game. New parser.commands._emit_command_telemetry(ctx, cmd, *, matched) fires one fail-open telemetry.emit('command', {matched, cmd, char_id?, typed?, sw?}) at two real chokepoints: top of CommandParser._execute (matched=True + canonical cmd.key, covers normal+movement paths) and the unknown branch of parse_and_dispatch (matched=False + typed word). Yields the command-frequency histogram (catalog C — dead feature = ~0 use), per-system engagement split, alias-vs-canonical usage, and the unknown-command 'huh?' friction rate (catalog D). Lean record (char_id only when char attached; typed only when != canonical; sw only when switches present). Keep-rate = use-site telemetry.command_sample tunable (default 1.0, additive YAML; relative histogram preserved under uniform sampling). No dispatch behaviour change, no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean; parser/commands.py + 1 tunables.yaml line. New tests/test_command_telemetry.py (12).

2026-06-02 (Drop 2 follow-up — respawn-grace consumer WIRED): Closed the flagged Drop-2 loose end. engine.death.get_respawn_grace_until (reader) + the grace_until writer in on_pc_death shipped in Drop 2, but the reader had ZERO callers — the anti-spawn-camp protection its docstring promised did nothing (infrastructure-complete-but-unwired; verified by grep). Wired the consumer into parser/combat_commands.py _apply_combat_wear's PC-death block: before on_pc_death finalizes a death, it now calls get_respawn_grace_until(ctx.db, c.id); if the victim is still inside their grace window, the killing blow is REFUSED — wound_level capped at INCAPACITATED (alive, downed; NOT MORTALLY_WOUNDED, so no bleed-out next round), re-synced to DB + session, player notified ([RESPAWN PROTECTION] ...), and on_pc_death (corpse / inventory drop / wound debuff / BH-insurance) skipped entirely. Otherwise the normal death path is unchanged. Reader fails OPEN to 0.0 so a read error never grants invulnerability; grace is only written on PvP deaths (killer_id present), so the window is a PvP-respawn protection (RESPAWN_GRACE_SECONDS = 60s); while protected, repeated blows just re-cap at INCAPACITATED until the window elapses. Scope = refuse the lethal blow during the window regardless of attacker type (simple, faithful to the docstring); can tighten to same-killer-only later. Tests: tests/test_drop2_respawn_grace_wiring.py (+8: 5 source-structure wiring pins [reader called, gated before on_pc_death, caps at INCAPACITATED, death path intact, no-throw-guarded] + 3 real-db reader-contract checks, skip-guarded on the db backend). Sandbox verification: 8 wiring + 16 Drop-2 reconciliation (no regression) + 113 death/combat (test_pg1_death_a/b, test_combat_mechanics, test_session54_combat_umbrella) all green after installing aiosqlite+bcrypt in the sandbox. PENDING on Brian's box: full ~4,854 suite; in-client PvP smoke (kill a PC, respawn, attempt re-kill within 60s -> blow turned aside, no second corpse). Note: combat death finalization is in parser/combat_commands.py _apply_combat_wear, NOT engine/combat.py (the earlier handoff said 'combat.py' loosely; the actual consumer site is the command layer where on_pc_death is gated).

2026-06-03 (Drop 3 B4 — gear insurance): Completed the policyholder side of insurance that Drop 2 death-reconciliation deferred. A one-shot policy: +insure buy debits a flat premium (GEAR_INSURANCE_PREMIUM=500, tunable) via adjust_credits('gear_insurance_premium', a sink); on the next lawless/contested death on_pc_death keeps the holder's loose loadout on them (instead of dropping to a re-lootable corpse) and consumes the policy; corpse still made but empty of their gear. KEY DESIGN CALL: RESTORATION model, NOT a credit indemnity — this death model sends gear to a re-lootable corpse, so a credit payout would be a suicide-faucet (die->re-loot->keep payout). No payout = nothing to farm = exploit-safe; premium is the only credit movement. Uninsured deaths still drop gear (sink survives). engine/gear_insurance.py (pure), parser/insurance_commands.py (+insure/buy/cancel, registered in game_server.py), db/database.py migration v39 (characters.gear_insured + allowlist; SCHEMA_VERSION 39), engine/death.py on_pc_death protection branch (fail-open). +16 tests (test_gear_insurance.py); Drop 2 death (16) + ledger pin (4) green. Refund-safe. Surfaces on @economy automatically. TUNABLE: flat premium for v1 — if everyone insures (eroding the gear-loss sink), raise it or scale by loadout value (valuation affects only fairness, never exploit-safety). SCHEMA NOTE: v39 — if a concurrent drop also claims v39, renumber. PENDING Brian: full Windows suite + smoke (+insure buy charges 500; lawless death keeps gear + consumes policy vs uninsured drops; secured no-op). Remaining Drop 3: B2 home/city prestige, B3 vanity/regalia, A3 spy intel-desk, A5 Hutt den, A8 entertainer audience-weighting, faction mission configs/commissary (A4).

2026-06-05 (Test-infra fix — run_all_tests.bat 'ERROR: usage: ... [file_or_dir]', reported from a Windows run): The runner unconditionally passed --timeout=120 --timeout-method=thread; those flags only exist when pytest-timeout is installed, and Brian's env didn't have it, so pytest rejected the whole invocation with a usage/unrecognized-arguments error and wrote no useful log (findstr then surfaced the 'ERROR: usage:' line). Reproduced the exact error in a clean env (pytest WITHOUT pytest-timeout). FIX: run_all_tests.bat now probes `python -c "import pytest_timeout"` and only adds the --timeout flags when it imports (errorlevel 0); otherwise it prints a one-line `pip install pytest-timeout` hint and runs WITHOUT the per-test timeout, so the suite runs either way and writes tests_output.log. Verified both branches in a clean env (absent -> flags dropped, 1 passed; present -> flags accepted, 1 passed). -o addopts= already neutralizes pytest.ini so the ini wasn't involved. run_regression_tests.bat does NOT pass --timeout (not affected; still uses the older Tee-Object redirection — separate lower-priority item, left unchanged). RECOMMEND Brian: pip install pytest-timeout (or -r requirements.txt) to restore hang-protection. Handoff: HANDOFF_run_all_tests_fix_2026-06-05.md.

Killing a wilderness-encounter creature lets the killer field-dress it: a Survival check (data-driven DC) yields a resource stack (organic hides/pelts; chemical for the crab water-sacs + DC-10 spor venom) into inventory.resources via crafting.add_resource. 7 creatures gained wired harvest blocks (hitcher_crab, magus, stalker_lizard, voroos[yield 2], wrix, tymp, spor_crawler[DC 10]); both live biomes (Dune Sea + Coruscant Underworld) balanced organic+chemical. ECONOMY ANALYSIS (Brian flagged flood risk post-audit): traced resource->value plumbing — raw resources have NO NPC buyer (the `sell` builtin takes equipped ITEMS only and refuses good crafts per econ v2 §1.3); resources monetize only P2P via escrow-backed vendor-droid buy orders (vendor_buy_order_escrow — money supply untouched) or are consumed by crafting. => spoils mint ZERO credits, NO inflation vector. DELIVERED 2026-06-20 (drop chargen-csp-frame-ancestors-2026-06-20). LIVE launch blocker found during Brian's playtest: new players could not create a character. The embedded chargen flow frames /chargen?embedded=1 in a same-origin <iframe> inside /play, but the T3.21 header hardening (server/web_client.py _SECURITY_HEADERS) set X-Frame-Options: DENY + CSP frame-ancestors 'none' app-wide, which blocked ALL framing incl. same-origin -> Chrome refused the iframe -> chargen never rendered. Fix: SAMEORIGIN + frame-ancestors 'self' (still blocks cross-origin clickjacking, allows the SPA's own iframe; correct in prod behind a TLS proxy too). test_t321_security_headers.py updated + regression guard added; 14 green. Needs a server restart to take effect. LESSON: security-header changes must be validated against the SPA's own same-origin iframe usage.

tier_1_active.title: skill_check_passed trigger-site decision
tier_1_active.resolution: Option 2: explicit `chain attempt` command. Shipped May 20 2026 per engine/chain_events.py docstring (line 52-79). Player types `chain attempt` at the chain step's authored location; the parser command (parser/chain_commands.py::ChainCommand) reads the active step via get_active_step_info, runs the authored perform_skill_check against the step's skill and difficulty, and dispatches the result to on_skill_check_passed. All 6 skill_check_passed completions in chains.yaml use this seam.
alternatives_rejected: Option 1: roll on step entry (silently, no player agency) — rejected; bad UX
Option 3: per-step attempt_on_command YAML schema — rejected; new schema surface for marginal gain
tier_1_active.rationale: Option 2 won on (a) player agency, (b) no new YAML schema, (c) discoverable via a dedicated command that later carries `chain status`, `chain skip`, etc.

tier_2_queued.spec: docs/design/dice_animation_and_ux_polish_2026-06-22.md. Animate the signature D6 roll (REAL pool + the exploding wild die) on DRAMATIC moments only, never gating pace/information; skippable + an Off/Minimal/Full toggle; real dice double as a D6 teaching tool (NPE). 'Dramatic' = a server-computed drama tier (0/1/2) at perform_skill_check + the combat resolver from signals already in hand (wild-die explosion/1, critical_success, outcome state-change, high-stakes category [Force/combat-decider/sabacc/PvP/chargen], difficulty+margin), then CLIENT-side rate-limited + preference-gated. Web-only; telnet unaffected; engine sends the drama field + the existing roll breakdown (EXTEND the funnel, no new system). Other candidates: sabacc card flips, credit/XP tick-up, map room-pan, combat hit/wound flash.
tier_2_queued.related_prelaunch: a small dramatic-only slice (Force powers + sabacc + the combat finishing blow), fully skippable, is a cheap first-impression differentiator that could land pre-launch; the full set + the other animations are post-launch
tier_2_queued.decisions: RESOLVED in the doc — Brian asked how 'dramatic' is determined; answer is the SIGNAL-BASED drama-tier scheme (NOT a hardcoded command list): f(wild-die, outcome-significance, criticality, category, difficulty+margin) classified at the funnel, then rate-limited on the client.

Also needs an item-mod-to-ship application surface — likely a parser command like 'install <item>' or admin-applies-to-ship.
tier_2_queued.engine_state: ALREADY WIRED + VERIFIED 2026-06-13 (drop 43) — the 'inert' claim was STALE (fixed earlier by CRAFT.P0.3/P0.4). The full loop exists end-to-end: both parts are output_type:component with stat_target/stat_boost/cargo_weight; the craft 'component' branch (parser/crafting_commands.py:1308) produces a type:ship_component item carrying quality; +ship/install (_install_mod, parser/space_commands.py:1616) is owner+docked-gated, READS the component quality, applies _quality_factor, caps via _MOD_MAX_*, runs an install skill check (fumble drops quality), writes systems.modifications; get_effective_stats (engine/starships.py:1488) applies the quality-scaled boost to live ship stats. The crafting-integration review's 'thread the INSTANCE quality, do not key off the bare key' trap is ALREADY handled correctly here (unlike the armor bug). No new mechanic was needed or built (would have been a redundant parallel system).
tier_2_queued.status_note: DONE-AS-ALREADY-WORKING (status above kept 'queued' historically; effectively resolved). The only real gap was VERIFICATION, now closed: tests/test_t5_ship_part_effects.py (5) proves craft-shape -> install -> quality-scaled effective-stat boost end-to-end, with quality genuinely consulted (regression guard vs the armor-decoration-bug class). The earlier 'build a new install mechanic' framing is moot.

tier_2_queued.title: T5 schematic discoverability UI in 'craft' command
tier_2_queued.design_doc: Existing parser/crafting_commands.py — extend the 'craft' command's schematic-list output to surface T5 entries with appropriate gating-info display (which T5 mat needed, current quality available in inventory, can_craft yes/no).
tier_2_queued.scope: Today the 'craft' command lists all schematics in a character's attributes.schematics. T5 schematics work for any character who has them in that list (via admin-grant or the future T2.DEF.t5_trainer_storyline path). The discoverability concern is: even with a schematic granted, the player doesn't see a clear 'you have q72 kyber, need q75 to craft this' diagnostic. Surface that.
tier_2_queued.closed_by: Crafting command repair + T5 discoverability drop
tier_2_queued.resolution_note: Closed by the 2026-05-31 crafting-command repair drop. The discoverability ask itself was Bug A: engine.crafting.check_resources now distinguishes QUANTITY-blocked from QUALITY-blocked failures, so a crafter holding 5x q72 kyber is told the grade is short (best q72, need q75+) instead of the misleading 'need 3x more'. While in the file, four adjacent schema-drift bugs in parser/crafting_commands.py were fixed: (B1) `schematics` listing read resource_requirements+skill -> 'Needs: none' for all 38 recipes, now reads components+skill_required with a [*] craftable flag; (B2) `resources` listing read r['amount'] -> KeyError crash, now reads quantity; (C) `craft` rolled a non-existent 'repair' skill, now rolls skill_required; (D, SEVERE) `craft` passed a float to resolve_craft -> AttributeError on every craft with materials in hand (command was non-functional end-to-end), now passes the SkillCheckResult. +13 tests (test_crafting_discoverability.py); 488 neighbor green.

pre_flight_findings: Finding 1: TODO.json::tech_debt named MAX_CLAIMS_PER_ZONE / MAX_CLAIMS_PER_ORG; actual constants in engine/territory.py:66-67 are CLAIM_MAX_PER_ZONE = 3 and CLAIM_MAX_TOTAL = 10. The surface list is corrected in this drop.

Finding 2: is_room_claimed_by has 6 consumers beyond engine/security.py::_apply_claim_upgrade: parser/faction_commands.py (armory gate, 2 sites), engine/territory.py (armory storage/withdraw, 3 internal sites), engine/sleeping.py (sleeping bonus, 1 site). SYN.1 must retarget or retire each of these 6 sites; the design doc estimate of ~1 sess still holds since each site is mechanical (region-scope swap) but the call-site count is recorded here so SYN.1's pre-flight doesn't re-discover it.

Finding 3: engine/player_cities.py::found_city is NOT anchor-agnostic at HEAD. Steps 7-10 of the validation chain hard-code the org's tier-5 HQ entry room → that room's zone_id → that zone's declared_security → org influence in that zone. SYN.4 rewrites all four steps to anchor on a wilderness region. The design doc's ~2 sess estimate for SYN.4 is consistent with this scope.

Finding 4: engine/player_cities.py::claim_room_for_city (city expansion) was added later than the design doc accounted for. SYN.4 retires this entirely — the per-room expansion model goes away when cities anchor on regions; expansion happens via wilderness landmark capture + building construction (SYN.9).

tier_2_queued.resolution: New wilderness-aware step 4 added to engine/security.py::get_effective_security per design §2.3. Two new helpers: _get_wilderness_region_state(room, db) reads default_security from the wilderness_regions registry + owner from region_ownership (SYN.1.a); _apply_wilderness_ownership(base, char, region_state) is the pure citadel-upgrade rule (LAWLESS → CONTESTED for owners, base stands for outsiders or unowned regions, CONTESTED stays CONTESTED — no double promotion). Step 4 is terminal for wilderness rooms (skips steps 5-6 room-prop fallback). _apply_claim_upgrade physically deleted; _finalize stripped of its claim_upgrade call. Director overlays (steps 1-3) still short-circuit step 4 — admin overrides win. City-map resolution path unchanged. tier_2_queued.title: Active harvest mechanic (first half of SYN.6)
tier_2_queued.resolution: Shipped engine/harvest.py (~510 LOC) + parser/harvest_command.py + 57 tests. Pure helpers (_yield_table_lookup, _apply_skill_margin, _quality_to_resource_quality, _compute_tax, compute_harvest_payout) + DB-touching perform_harvest. 6-row yield table per design §2.5.2 verbatim (security × influence-tier). Survival skill check at DC 6 (WEG Easy). 30-min per-region cooldown with per-region namespacing. 15% non-owner credit tax routed to owner treasury via existing adjust_org_treasury seam. Resource stacks land in inventory.resources via engine.crafting.add_resource — same storage crafting already reads from, no schema migration. Region-quality multiplier is a seam (_get_region_quality) returning 1.0 in SYN.6.a; SYN.6.b is a one-function swap. T5 rare hits grant +1 'rare' stack at q100 (top of band) so existing crafting sees them immediately and T5 recipes can gate on min_quality ≥ 95 when they land. Influence: NONE — pinned by TestPerformHarvestNoInfluence per §4.25 wilderness-only-influence invariant + design §2.7 reward table. 10/10 deterministic runs after fixing the test fixture's skill field placement (skills are a top-level char dict field, not nested in attributes).

tier_2_queued.title: Weekly region-quality variance + Director resource-outlook digest (second half of SYN.6)
tier_2_queued.resolution: Shipped engine/region_quality.py (~290 LOC) — full weekly-quality subsystem: schema (region_quality table, composite PK region_slug+resource_type), pure helpers (_iso_year_week canonical 'YYYY-Www', _compute_weekly_multiplier 0.7..1.3 with 2-decimal rounding, _outlook_summary best/worst with stable tie-breaking), DB-touching wrappers (roll_region_quality idempotent per ISO week, get_region_quality_for fail-soft dict[type,float], get_outlook with optional org filter, tick_weekly_region_quality iterating SELECT DISTINCT wilderness_region_id FROM rooms — broader than region_ownership so un-owned regions get rolls too since SYN.6.a maps them to fallback yields). Schema bootstrap is per-feature (ensure_region_quality_schema) not a SCHEMA_VERSION bump — mirrors SYN.1.a pattern. compute_harvest_payout in engine/harvest.py now polymorphic on quality: accepts float (SYN.6.a back-compat) OR dict[type, float] (SYN.6.b consumer); new stack_qualities result field. _get_region_quality seam swapped from return 1.0 to delegating into engine.region_quality.get_region_quality_for with fail-soft fallback. parser/faction_commands.py new _cmd_resource_outlook subcommand (aliases: outlook, resources) with green/yellow/dim color-grading by best-multiplier. Hourly tick (region_quality_weekly_tick) with per-region per-week ISO 8601 anchor — matches city_maintenance_tick pattern, simpler than wiring cron on interval-based scheduler. 38 new tests across 10 sections, 95 tests across SYN.6.a+SYN.6.b combined, 5/5 deterministic runs. Adjacent SYN family (394) + faction tests (94) all green.

Module-level transient state keyed by region_slug (restart wipes, matches engine.space_anomalies pattern). 5 CW-correct templates: stranded_clone_scout (Republic, Medicine/Survival), salvage_cache (Technical/Survival), wounded_animal (Survival/Brawling), tusken_party (Blaster/Melee), crashed_cis_probe (Technical/Knowledge). All templates ship simplified combat-resolution via skill check rather than full NPC spawn — proper NPC spawn for templates 3-4 (wounded_animal, tusken_party) defers to a post-launch polish drop. parser/anomaly_commands.py NEW: AnomaliesCommand (lists active anomalies in caller's wilderness region with ~Nm-left countdown) + InvestigateCommand (resolves anomaly with skill check). server/tick_handlers_economy.py: wilderness_anomaly_tick wrapper. server/game_server.py: scheduler registration (interval=3600, offset=1500). Hourly cadence + 0.4 spawn-chance per region per tick → expected ~2.5h avg interval per region (matches design 'every 2-3 hours per region'). MAX_PER_REGION=2 cap. TIER1_DURATION_SECS=1800 (30 min). +5 influence to resolver's faction on success. News broadcast on each spawn via session_mgr.broadcast (best-effort). One-shot resolution semantics: failed skill check still consumes the anomaly and grants reduced fail_reward (anti-farm + low frustration). 41 new tests across 10 sections, 5/5 deterministic. New per-template fields: 'regions: [...]' (region-tag list; REGION_ANY sentinel for universal) and 'resolution: skill|combat'. _pick_template now filters by region; returns None for unknown regions (system stays silent in un-templated regions rather than spawning random content). Combat-mode templates declare 'combat_npcs: [...]' specs (archetype + tier + species + name_pool + weapon + behavior + personality) — one real NPC spawned per spec via db.create_npc; ai_config_json tagged with is_anomaly_target + anomaly_id. New helpers: award_combat_anomaly_reward (the kill-hook entry point; decrements live-NPC list, fires payout on last-hostile death), find_anomaly_globally (cross-region anomaly lookup for the kill hook), _prune_expired_region_with_cleanup (deletes surviving NPCs from expired combat anomalies), _gate_investigate + _resolve_anomaly_skill + _resolve_anomaly_combat (split resolution paths). parser/combat_commands.py: anomaly kill hook inserted between bounty kill hook and WoW.3a kill credit; mirrors bounty pattern (reads ai_config_json.is_anomaly_target on dying NPC, calls award_combat_anomaly_reward, surfaces [ANOMALY CLEARED] line via session_mgr.find_by_character; attribution via c.last_attacker_id, same chain as bounty + WoW.3a). parser/anomaly_commands.py: InvestigateCommand branches on result.mode — combat-mode shows long_desc + 'use attack' nudge; skill-mode shows the roll + verdict + resource grants. Resolution mix: 4 skill (stranded_clone_scout, salvage_cache, crashed_cis_probe, factory_cache) + 6 combat. tier_2_queued.scope: 5 CW-correct Tier 2 templates (3 Dune Sea + 2 Coruscant; region parity from SYN.7.a.fix): downed_republic_acclamator (3 phases — CIS scout element → heavy response → salvage commander; T5 drops weapons_capacitor_core), hutt_smuggling_convoy (2 phases — outriders → convoy heavies; T5 drops weapons_capacitor_core), cis_commando_deployment (3 phases — BX vanguard → heavy support → tactical droid commander; named item loot: T-Series Tactical Droid Command Module), maze_predator_outbreak (3 phases — scout pack → main pack → alpha; T5 drops composite_chitin), coruscant_gang_war (2 phases — first volley → boss engagement; named item loot: Black Sun Vigo Signet Ring). Cadence: 6h tick × 0.20 chance → ~30h avg interval. Per-region cap 1 (T2 is the headline event). 2h duration. +20 influence (mid of design's 15-25 band).
tier_2_queued.resolution: Extended engine/wilderness_anomalies.py from ~1100 LOC to ~1500 LOC. New TIER2_TEMPLATES catalog with 5 templates, each with `phases: [...]` + optional `named_loot: {...}`. Multi-phase combat dispatch: investigate spawns ONLY phase 0 NPCs; killing the last NPC of phase N triggers _advance_to_next_phase which spawns phase N+1's NPCs (or fires final payout if at last phase). New helpers: _spawn_combat_npcs (extracted shared spawn loop), _advance_to_next_phase (phase advancement with intro broadcast), _payout_combat_anomaly (split T1 single-char from T2 multi-char payout), _grant_named_loot (resource + item shapes). Tier 2 payout distributes credits/resources to ALL characters in anchor room (split equally for credits; resource list shared); killer alone gets influence (+20 to faction) and named loot. WildernessAnomaly dataclass extended with tier (default 1), current_phase, total_phases property, is_final_phase property. _pick_template signature gained tier=int parameter (1 or 2); selects from TIER1_TEMPLATES vs TIER2_TEMPLATES disjointly. spawn_anomaly_for_region gained tier kwarg with per-tier duration + cap. New tick: tick_tier2_wilderness_anomalies (separate from Tier 1 tick — caps, durations, chances all independent). parser/combat_commands.py kill hook extended: detects tier from payout, surfaces per-participant [ANOMALY CLEARED] lines for Tier 2; killer gets +inf line + [NAMED LOOT] line; passes session_mgr through to award_combat_anomaly_reward for phase-intro broadcasts. server/tick_handlers_economy.py: tier2_wilderness_anomaly_tick wrapper. server/game_server.py: scheduler registration (interval=21600, offset=3300 — won't collide with Tier 1's offset=1500).

tier_2_queued.scope: 4 CW-correct Tier 3 templates per design verbatim: krayt_dragon (Dune Sea, 3 phases — juvenile pack guards → elder krayt → enraged krayt; trophy: Krayt Dragon Scale; scaled T5 mat: deep_dune_iron q80), maze_predator_apex (Coruscant Underworld, 3 phases — pack vanguard → the apex → frenzy; trophy: Maze Apex Fang; scaled T5 mat: composite_chitin q80), crashed_separatist_capital_ship (REGION_ANY, 3 phases — perimeter security → heavy response → tactical command; trophy: Salvaged Separatist Hull Plate; scaled T5 mat: weapons_capacitor_core q80), republic_lost_patrol (REGION_ANY, 3 phases — outer picket → interrogation detail → final reinforcements; trophy: Republic Patrol Insignia; scaled T5 mat: weapons_capacitor_core q80). Cadence: daily tick × 0.10 chance → ~10-day avg per region (mid of design's 7-14d). Per-region cap 1. 8h duration. +50 influence to killing-blow faction.
tier_2_queued.resolution: Extended engine/wilderness_anomalies.py from ~1500 LOC to ~2000 LOC. New TIER3_TEMPLATES catalog with 4 templates, each adding trophy_per_participant + scaled_t5_mat fields. New helpers: _grant_trophy (adds item to inventory['items'] with is_trophy:True + is_anomaly_loot:True), _distribute_scaled_t5_mat (floor(N/4) pieces ranked by kill count descending; killer wins ties; minimum 1 piece consolation for small teams). New tick_tier3_wilderness_anomalies parallel to T1/T2 ticks. parser/combat_commands.py kill hook extended: detects tier=3; surfaces [WORLD BOSS DEFEATED] headline, [TROPHY] line per participant, [T5 MATERIAL] line for top scaled-T5 recipients with kill count. server/tick_handlers_economy.py: tier3_wilderness_anomaly_tick wrapper. server/game_server.py: scheduler registration (interval=86400, offset=7200 — won't collide with T1 offset=1500 or T2 offset=3300). Also fixed: total_phases property previously checked tier==2; now tier>=2.

tier_2_queued.title: Player-constructed building system
tier_2_queued.scope: engine/buildings.py (~1150 LOC) with 5 categories per design literal: residence (5000cr + 5 metal + 5 organic; 50-item personal storage), crafting_station (8000cr + 10 metal + 5 composite; +1D crafting bonus lookup), commerce_stall (6000cr + 8 metal; 50/50 owner/city vendor split lookup), garrison_annex (10000cr + 15 metal + 5 chemical; spawns 2 defending NPCs at completion), cultural_hall (7500cr + 8 metal + 5 organic; +1 daily CP lookup). Construction takes 24 real-time hours; demolish refunds 25% materials; same-owner rebuild gets 10% discount. Mayor evict has 2-day notice. Slot capacity per landmark defaults to 2; force-resonant landmarks have 0 slots.
tier_2_queued.resolution: Shipped engine/buildings.py with full substrate: ensure_schema (idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS), construct_building (validates rank 3+ in city's owning org + slot free + materials + credits; deducts on start; creates row with status='under_construction' + completion_ts=now+24h), demolish_building (owner-initiated; 25% refund if operational; no refund if under_construction; cleans up garrison NPCs), evict_building (mayor-initiated; sets evict_after_ts=now+2d; double-evict rejected), tick_building_construction (transitions under_construction→operational at 24h with garrison NPC spawn; transitions operational+evict-notice→evicted at 2-day expiry with NPC cleanup), 4 effect-lookup helpers (get_crafting_station_bonus, get_cultural_hall_in_room, get_commerce_stall_in_room, get_residence_for_owner), residence_store_item + residence_take_item (owner-only 50-item cap). parser/player_building_commands.py with PlayerBuildingCommand dispatch (7 subcommands: construct, demolish, evict, list, inspect, store, take). Filename chosen to avoid collision with existing parser/building_commands.py (admin world-building, @dig/@tunnel/etc — unrelated system). server/tick_handlers_economy.py: building_construction_tick wrapper. server/game_server.py: import + ensure_schema at boot + scheduler register (interval=300s = 5min, offset=120) + register_player_building_commands at parser bootstrap.

server/game_server.py (modified — import + ensure_schema at boot + scheduler register + parser register)
tests/test_syn9_player_buildings.py (new — ~1100 LOC, 54 tests across 11 sections, 5/5 deterministic)
depends_on: engine.player_cities (DONE — get_city_for_room used to validate which city owns a landmark)
engine.organizations (DONE — get_membership used for rank check)
engine.npc_generator + ai.npc_brain (DONE — garrison NPC spawn at completion)
what_deferred_within_scope: Consumer integration of effect-lookup helpers. The substrate ships the 4 lookup functions but does NOT wire them into consumers — engine.crafting still does not consult get_crafting_station_bonus during craft rolls, engine.cp_engine still does not consult get_cultural_hall_in_room during daily CP tick, no vendor-surface command consumes get_commerce_stall_in_room. Lookup helpers ready for SYN.10 polish or post-launch wiring. This is the 'seam discipline' pattern (architecture v50): ship the contract first with no consumer, fail-loud on missing consumer rather than silent fallback.

Commerce stall full vendor surface (player-vendor commands for listing items, accepting buyer credits, 50/50 split with city treasury). Substrate detects the commerce_stall building exists in a room; the vendor command surface (analogous to bounty board or sabacc) is deferred.
Cultural hall time-tracking. The design says '5+ minutes here'; tracking per-char time-in-room requires either a periodic tick poll or hooking room-move events. Deferred.
Donate-to-org flow. construct_building has a `donate_to_org` parameter that sets owning_org_id on the building row; the parser surface doesn't expose it yet. Substrate supports it.
Building HP / combat damage. The schema has an hp column; nothing decrements it. Tier 3 anomaly events / contest damage as design mentions for destruction is deferred.

tier_2_queued.title: Display integration + launch polish
tier_2_queued.priority_note: FINAL in SYN sequence. Architecture v49 rollup opens next. T2.ECON.review opens.
tier_2_queued.scope: Display integration: engine/territory_display.py (new, ~890 LOC) with web-UI-contract data dict (get_region_data_block) + ANSI CLI renderer (get_region_look_block) + faction-scoped renderers (get_faction_contests_lines/data, get_faction_resource_outlook_lines/data) + 6 news-format helpers (ownership_change, contest_start, contest_resolve, anomaly_defeat, building_completion, building_demolition). parser/region_commands.py (new) — +region [slug] command. Region info block auto-injects into wilderness `look` between security/movement and adjacent terrain. +faction contest + +faction resource_outlook subcommands wired into existing FactionUmbrellaCommand. News broadcast hooks added at: anomaly defeat (T1/T2/T3 payout), claim_region/unclaim_region return-dict `news` field, garrison_annex completion (global broadcast; other building categories stay owner-only). Contest start/resolve broadcasts predated SYN.10 with [REGION CONTEST]/[REGION DEFENDED]/[REGION SEIZED] tags — design-equivalent, not retouched.
tier_2_queued.resolution: Substantial UI-pivot-bridge content shipped. Data contract (get_region_data_block) is the canonical web-UI surface. ANSI palette centralized in engine/territory_display.py constants (_RED/_YELLOW/_GREEN/_CYAN/_MAGENTA/_BOLD/_DIM/_ITALIC) — web theme maps these to semantic CSS classes. All *_lines functions accept ansi=False for plain-text rendering (test snapshots, log dumps, structured data consumers). Failure-tolerant by design: any sub-section failure (missing wilderness_regions row, missing region_quality data, no contest active) silently no-ops so the render never breaks the wilderness look itself.
files_touched: engine/territory_display.py (new — ~890 LOC: structured data + CLI renderer + 6 news formatters)
parser/builtin_commands.py (modified — region info block injected in _look_wilderness)
engine/territory.py (modified — claim_region/unclaim_region return dict gains optional 'news' field)
engine/wilderness_anomalies.py (modified — _broadcast_anomaly_defeat called at end of every payout path)
engine/buildings.py (modified — garrison_annex completion fires global broadcast)
what_deferred_within_scope: Web HUD region-ownership + contest-countdown panel (design §3.12 calls it 'post-launch polish; CLI suffices for launch'). The data contract get_region_data_block ships as substrate; the upcoming UI work consumes it.
News-digest persistence (current implementation is real-time broadcast only; no log of news events for catch-up display when offline). Useful but design-deferred.

Faction influence dashboard region-keyed rewrite. The design also asks for the existing `faction influence` command to be 'rewritten for region-keyed display.' The +region command + auto-overlay covers most of this need; the existing dashboard still renders per-zone. Deferred to UI pivot since the web HUD will likely replace the CLI dashboard entirely.

Building completion broadcast for non-garrison categories. residence/crafting_station/commerce_stall/cultural_hall completions stay owner-only on purpose (private investment signal). Could be configurable later.
ui_pivot_handoff: engine/territory_display.py is the contract module for the upcoming UI work. All structured-dict functions return stable shapes (documented in module docstring + function docstrings).

ANSI palette consolidation: web theme maps 8 color tags (_RED/_YELLOW/_GREEN/_CYAN/_MAGENTA/_BOLD/_DIM/_ITALIC) to semantic classes (security_lawless, security_contested, security_secured, accent_heading, contest_panel, emphasis, subtle, descriptive).
6 news-format helpers ship pre-formatted news strings the web HUD news ticker can consume directly.
+region command surfaces the same data CLI-side; the web HUD has the option to either reuse the data accessor or render its own.

tier_2_queued.resolution: Brian delegated the call (use judgment, complete implementation). Migrated all 8 Coruscant vertical-tier zones to the §3.1 function taxonomy: coruscant_senate→senate_district, coruscant_temple→jedi_temple, coruscant_upper→monumental_district, coruscant_midlevels→commercial_district, coruscant_works→commercial_district (MERGED — §3 mandates exactly one contested hand-built zone, so no off-catalog industrial zone; the Works is CSF-patrolled infrastructure = Secured/commercial), coruscant_gilded_cage→entertainment_district, coruscant_lower→southern_underground (the single Contested hand-built zone, the Cantina-Sanctuary archetype), coruscant_underworld unchanged (Lawless). Applied §3 security across ALL THREE schemes that had drifted apart: zones.yaml properties.security (canonical, read by security.py), coruscant.yaml per-room security_level (27 secured/13 contested/1 lawless), and the hardcoded _ZONE_SECURITY dict in engine/housing_lots_provider.py. Renamed across 32 files + zones.yaml keys + the npcs_drop_c2 roster file (coruscant_lower→southern_underground, on-disk rename). validate_world: 0 errors; 72/72 unique room slugs (merge created no collisions); no leftover old refs. Guard: tests/test_t2_5_and_t2_11b_morale_and_rezone.py. NOTE for Windows: the old roster file npcs_drop_c2_coruscant_lower.yaml must be removed (Expand-Archive can't delete a renamed file).

tier_2_queued.context: E2 wired the passive world-event multipliers. The remaining 6 effects are FLAGS that each ENABLE a NEW player interaction and need a design call before any code: (1) contraband_scan (SECURITY_CHECKPOINT) — sale-time scan RISK when selling contraband: what is the consequence (forfeit goods? fine? arrest chance?) and at which sale site? (2) rare_vendor (MERCHANT_ARRIVAL) — a vendor offers RARE stock while active: which items, from which pool, at what price, on which vendor surface? (3) hutt_auction + criminal_rep_gate:30 (HUTT_AUCTION) — an AUCTION interaction gated on criminal reputation >= 30: new command? what's auctioned, bidding model, where does rep come from? (4) krayt_bounty (KRAYT_SIGHTING) — a special high-value BOUNTY/hunt becomes available: bounty-board hook or new target spawn? (5) brawl_active (CANTINA_BRAWL) — a joinable cantina BRAWL: join command, resolution (skill check?), reward/risk? (6) distress_active (DISTRESS_SIGNAL) — an answerable DISTRESS beacon: accept command, what's the encounter/reward? Each should reuse existing systems where possible (bounty board, vendor-droid market, reputation, encounter pipeline) rather than bespoke mechanics.

tier_2_queued.status: IMPLEMENTATION IN PROGRESS (2026-06-10, drop 2): UI-7 WEB ONBOARDING DESIGNED + SHIPPED this drop per Brian's retention mandate ('handled well, and thoroughly'). Producer = chain_events.build_onboarding_state layered on get_active_step_info (extended ADDITIVELY — chain status/attempt unaffected); push = session._hud_sidebar_onboarding with the _last_chain_step graduation-once memo (reconnect after graduation = silence). KEY AUDIT FINDING: the tutorial SYSTEM was already fully live (9 chains, chargen-start, 11 hooks, graduation, chain status parity) — get_active_step_info's docstring claimed 'Used by web HUD' but nothing did; UI-7 fills that seam. Phase 2 candidates logged (in_step_location hint, tutorial_v2 elective rail, missions modal) — flagged, not half-built. Remaining: UI-8 crafting (after T2.CRAFT.integration_design_pass); commissary fold-in gated on UI-4b.commissary_vendor_merge (pending Brian). --- prior same-day drop 1: UI-5 bounty board + UI-6 objective/juice shipped; _hud_active_jobs bounty-branch fix; protocol ledger v1_4 reconstructed after the 06-07 phantom. --- prior 2026-06-07: UI-1..UI-4b + equipment untangle shipped. Web UI is plausibly make-or-break for the target audience (Brian: 'Different audience, not the same MUSHers I grew up with' — matches Claude's original web-first call).
tier_2_queued.goal: Produce a DETAILED package that Claude Design can consume to expand the web SPA UI for gameplay surfaces that are currently text-command-only. Claude (this project) knows the gameplay; Claude Design does NOT — so the package must teach it each surface: what the system does, the current text commands/flows, what server data is already exposed (and what needs a protocol addition), the proposed web interaction model, and explicitly what stays text.
tier_2_queued.hard_principle: Posing / speaking / emotes / free-text RP STAY TEXT — that free-text channel is the soul of a MUSH and the web UI must augment it, never replace it. Web UI is for MECHANICAL STATE and STRUCTURED interactions (browse/select/confirm/manage), shown alongside the RP text stream.
tier_2_queued.reference_point: The just-shipped vendor-interaction UX is the pattern to extend. Inventory is the obvious adjacent surface; Brian's bet (shared by Claude) is there is a LOT more.
candidate_surfaces_claude_enumerated: Inventory + equip/unequip + containers + get/drop/give (Brian's named adjacent surface).
Character sheet — already has SPA tier renderers + a sheet_redesign_design; EXTEND, don't restart (attributes/skills/Force skills/wounds/CP/FP/DSP/equipment).

Combat HUD — initiative order, range bands, wound/stun track, the NEW special-attack conditions (poison stacks, grapple/constriction restraint), action declaration (attack/dodge/parry/full-dodge/flee) as buttons. Posing/called shots stay text; the mechanical state is ideal for UI. High-impact, adjacent to just-shipped combat work.
Space + SPACE COMBAT — ship position/range/shields/hull/weapon arcs/sensors/hyperspace/cargo. Space combat especially benefits from a spatial UI.
Crafting + mining/harvest — recipe browser, material counts, build queue + progress (gundark_crafting_integration, sourcebook_mining_crafting_exp).
Economy — banking/ledger, the COMING +market (Drop 5), the bounty-hunter board, org treasury, P2P transfers. Markets/boards/banking are classic web surfaces.
Force-power palette — list + activate, control/sense/alter grouping.
CP spend / training + the milestone meter (progression_gates).
Org/faction — roster, ranks, reputation, treasury dashboards (faction_reputation, organizations_factions).
Territory control — map overlay of zone control state (map/POI work already exists; extend it).
Player city / housing / shop OWNER dashboards — plots, buildings, construction, stock, pricing.
Padawan/Master pairing + Jedi-Village & Spacer quest journals.
Tutorial / onboarding — a web onboarding flow is one of the biggest wins for the new (non-MUSHer) audience.

Medical/healing/respawn status; channels/mail/news/BBS (classic web surfaces); sabacc-den minigame + entertainer performance; security-zone + DIFFICULTY indicators (ties to T2.DIFF); minimap/navigation.

INVESTIGATE + FIX the scroll-on-move behavior, and evaluate whether a structured room header/panel is the better long-term answer than relying on scroll. (Possible standalone bug — could be triaged earlier if it bites in testing.)

Workflow = (1) Claude produces the package, (2) Brian prioritizes, (3) implementation drops follow. Surface the surface-list to Brian for additions/cuts before writing the full package.

tier_2_queued.title: Difficulty tiers / zoned content progression (DESIGN) — newbie vs mid vs end-game areas (Brian floated, 2026-06-06)
tier_2_queued.effort: medium-large (design + a short doc, then world-data + tiered spawn-tables + map/zone UI surfacing)
tier_2_queued.status: DESIGN DONE + DIFF.1 SHIPPED 2026-06-13 (drop 28). Six sub-decisions resolved in docs/design/difficulty_tiers_design_v1.md (difficulty=separate axis from security; 4 bands Frontier/Settled/Contested Marches/Deep Wilds; advisory+soft-gate; per-world tier map; space lanes tiered; frontier!=lawless + reward multiplier). DIFF.1 engine axis (engine/threat_band.py) + world-loader validator shipped behavior-neutral (default SETTLED everywhere). DIFF.2 SHIPPED 2026-06-13 (drop 29): all 37 zones labeled (additive), look header tag + +threat command. DIFF.3 SHIPPED 2026-06-13 (drop 30): tiered wilderness-encounter eligibility (min_band/max_band) — Frontier tiles draw trivial fauna, Deep Wilds unlock minibosses; authored band bounds on Coruscant maze_ambush + Dune Sea tusken_war_party. DIFF.4 SHIPPED 2026-06-13 (drop 31) + RATIFIED by Brian 2026-06-13: threat-band reward multiplier at the bounty payout (scales by the target room band; Frontier 0.6x, Wilds 2.0x; rides the existing bounty faucet). The 0.6/1.0/1.4/2.0 multipliers are CONFIRMED as launch values (retune from telemetry post-launch). DIFF.5 SHIPPED 2026-06-16 (drop diff5-area-map-band, Sonnet loop): area_map.py emits band field (room→zone inheritance, default settled) + client CSS label tinting (frontier=teal, contested-marches=orange, wilds=red; settled=default amber). ALL 5 DIFF ITEMS COMPLETE. Optional follow-ups (non-blocking, post-launch): extend reward_multiplier(band) to mission/encounter faucets; band-crossing banner + Wilds soft-gate.
tier_2_queued.blocking: Pre-launch. Independent of but CONNECTED to T2.UIPKG (zone difficulty indicators are a UI surface). Brian invited counter-proposals ('if you have something better I'm all ears').
tier_2_queued.problem_brian: No difficulty levels today. New players should be able to experience combat (and space combat) in a NEWBIE area, mid-game players in another, end-game in another. Wilderness should have SECTIONS with KNOWN lines (players see where the tiers are). PC cities should probably only sit in mid-to-high difficulty areas.
tier_2_queued.claude_recommendation: EXTEND, don't add (consistent with the 'Lane D plugs into existing faction-intent machinery' discipline): ride the EXISTING security-zone model + wilderness region/biome machinery rather than build a parallel difficulty system. Attach a threat/difficulty BAND to zones (e.g. Newcomer / Seasoned / Veteran, or a numeric threat rating), SURFACED on the map + room/zone header (the UI tie-in). Gate creature-spawn danger and space-patrol threat by zone tier — the Lane-A creatures already carry a danger profile, so build TIERED spawn tables over them. Restrict PC-city placement to mid+ tiers so newbie areas stay controlled/protected. Wire into the security/territory-control overlay already built.
open_decisions_for_brian: Tier taxonomy + count (named bands vs numeric threat rating).
Is difficulty == security level, or a separate parallel axis layered on zones?
How visibly to mark the lines — hard gates (can't enter) vs advisory (warned but allowed)?
Which of the 6 launch worlds host which tiers (Tatooine/Nar Shaddaa/Coruscant/Kuat/Kamino/Geonosis)?
Space-lane tiering — do space routes get the same bands?
Are newbie tiers PvP-safe (intersect with the +pvp opt-in already shipped)?
tier_2_queued.next_step: Once Brian picks a direction, write a short difficulty_tiers_design doc, then sequence the world-data + spawn-table + UI work (after Lanes A-F, alongside / feeding the UI package).

tier_2_queued.title: Crafting + harvesting integration design pass — holistic re-examination of crafting/economy integration across all systems (Brian, 2026-06-07)
tier_2_queued.lane: design (produce the doc) → implementation/wiring drops follow
tier_2_queued.effort: large (full cross-system design pass, then a sequence of wiring drops)
tier_2_queued.status: queued — SEQUENCED AFTER T2.UIPKG.claude_design_handoff (Brian: 'let's put that on todo after this UI update')
tier_2_queued.blocking: AFTER T2.UIPKG. Tightly connected to Lane C (gated gear catalog), Drop-5 (farming controls — faucets+sinks land together), the economy audit line (T2.ECON.*), and the deferred T5 items (T2.DEF.t5_*). Brian's D1 'eye to Phase C' points here.
tier_2_queued.problem_brian: Crafting is a MAJOR economy driver but was implemented EARLY; a lot of systems have been added/changed since crafting + harvesting first landed (active harvest, creature spoils, the 8 sourcebook gear extractions, faucets/sinks discipline, vendor sell-surface, faction commissary, T5 schematics). Brian suspects extracted/intended content meant to BUFF crafting was never wired ('I don't think we added the items [that] ... buffed crafting'). Crafting needs its OWN design pass — look at ALL the systems and make sure crafting is integrated well, not a patchwork. 'Let's get it right.'
tier_2_queued.goal: Audit the crafting + harvesting touchpoints and the GEAR-SOURCE GRAPH end-to-end. Anti-phantom: confirm every material / recipe / gear family has BOTH a producer and a consumer, and that extracted sourcebook crafting content is actually reachable in play. Re-integrate crafting cleanly with the CURRENT economy (faucets/sinks, vendor/commissary, harvest spoils + quality ceilings, Lane C gear families, T5 schematics). Produce a design doc FIRST, then sequence the wiring drops.
scope_candidates: Recipe/material graph completeness — every recipe's inputs have a real acquisition path; every harvestable/lootable resource feeds at least one recipe or sink.

The harvest → resource → craft → use/sink loop end-to-end (engine/harvest.py, engine/crafting.py, engine/creature_spoils.py): spoils quality ceiling (65) vs T5_MIN_QUALITY (75) still correct after any new gear.
Lane C gear families (purchasable / craftable / lootable) routing — which families are craftable, what they consume, and the Drop-5 faucet/sink pairing.
T5 schematic acquisition — fold T2.DEF.t5_trainer_storyline / t5_discoverability / t5_ship_part_items / t5_drop_hooks into this pass (they are all crafting-integration loose ends).
Unwired sourcebook crafting content sweep — the gear/material content extracted from the 8 sourcebooks that was meant to enrich crafting but may never have been wired (Brian's suspicion).
Crafting UI surface — coordinate with the T2.UIPKG crafting panel (recipe browser / material counts / build queue) so design + UI land coherently.
tier_2_queued.claude_todo_flag: DESIGN PASS FIRST (doc), then build — per the standing 're-think before building' rule. Surface findings + the integration plan to Brian before wiring drops.
tier_2_queued.ref: gundark_crafting_integration_design_v1.md; sourcebook_mining_crafting_exp_design_v1.md; economy_design_v02-1.md; economy_audit_v2.md; sourcebook_enrichment_roadmap_v1.md (Lane C); Guide_07_Crafting.md; Guide_06_Economy.md

tier_2_queued.title: Crafted quality -> combat for ARMOR + CONSUMABLES (Option A, Brian-greenlit) — DONE
tier_2_queued.lane: engine (combat/equipment + crafting) — HIGH collision; sequence carefully
tier_2_queued.effort: armor half DONE (drop 44); consumable half ~3-4h once the potency-model design doc lands
tier_2_queued.status: DONE 2026-06-13. ARMOR half drop 44 (crafted_armor_soak_pips, cap +2/floor -1, folded at the combat soak site). CONSUMABLE half drop 45 (crafted_consumable_potency_pips cap +1 — tighter for the small stim base; storage migration bare-int -> {count,quality} with lazy-coerce + max-on-recraft; consume_consumable kept a bool, new get_consumable_quality reader; stims scale buff magnitude, medpacs get a First-Aid roll reliability bonus, heal stays discrete). Both hard-capped, no power creep (q95 stim < next tier base, pinned). Design: docs/design/consumable_quality_potency_v1.md.
tier_2_queued.design_doc: Decision in design_calls_pending_brian CRAFT.quality_combat_read_armor_consumables (Option A); crafting-integration review HANDOFF_crafting_integration_review_2026-06-13.md items 3-5.
tier_2_queued.scope: Make crafted quality affect combat for armor + consumables the way it already does for weapons (engine/items.py crafted_combat_pips, Drop 19). ARMOR: get_armor_protection (engine/character.py:457) currently reads the bare worn_armor KEY -> a q95 vest soaks like q40. Read the worn-armor INSTANCE quality and fold a HARD-CAPPED quality delta into soak (mirror the weapon +1D pip-cap so no power creep). BUNDLE with TD.EQUIPMENT_CHARACTER_HOLDS_KEYS_NOT_INSTANCES (get_armor_protection needs the instance). CONSUMABLES: thread quality into potency (heal amount / buff magnitude), capped; touches the consumable storage shape (count -> quality-bearing) at parser/crafting_commands.py:1296 + medical_commands.py; migration-tolerant read; sequence AFTER in-flight crafting edits settle.
tier_2_queued.collision_note: ARMOR HALF (drop 44) did NOT bundle the equipment migration (primitive-threading instead). CONSUMABLE HALF touches engine/buffs.py + parser/crafting_commands.py + parser/medical_commands.py — sequence after any in-flight crafting/medical edits; not concurrent.
tier_2_queued.balance_note: Power-creep-sensitive. Cap the quality delta (e.g. the +1D / +3-pip weapon precedent) so a q95 item is BETTER but not game-warping vs vendor baseline. Consider deciding armor and consumable caps separately (potion-potency is the riskier knob).
tier_2_queued.armor_half_resolution: Drop 44. The armor decoration gap (q95 vest soaked like q40) is closed WITHOUT the equipment-instance migration: instead of making get_armor_protection take an instance, the soak pip is computed ONCE in from_db_dict (where the equipment JSON is still live) and cached as a primitive int on the Character (Drop-19 weapon-pip isolation pattern), then folded into armor_pool at engine/combat.py _apply_damage before Strength-add. get_armor_protection UNCHANGED (gundark registry pins safe). NO bundling with TD.EQUIPMENT_CHARACTER_HOLDS_KEYS_NOT_INSTANCES needed — the primitive-threading gets correctness without the multi-day refactor. Funnel reuses _quality_band_pips verbatim; armor has no experiment axis (weapons.yaml has no protection_mod) so cap is +2 not +3.
tier_2_queued.consumable_half_note: DONE drop 45. Refined the design doc's consume_consumable return-type change to a separate get_consumable_quality reader (less blast radius — consume_consumable stays bool, no caller ripple).

All satisfy is_huntable_mob(): hostile=true, no special-reward markers. Original characters per Q1, era-clean, WEG R&E D6 stats (WEG40120 absent in tree). Numeric values picked during feature shipping where Brian has NOT explicitly signed off. Each entry names the value, where it lives in code/data, what it currently is, what alternative values were considered, and the smallest possible test or playtest that would confirm or refute the pick.
Updated at end of every drop alongside CHANGELOG.md. New entries appended as SYN.7+ drops introduce new tuning knobs.

The whole-game economist pass (T2.ECON.review) RAN on 2026-05-31 (SW_MUSH_Economy_Audit_FINAL.md) and is closed. These remaining numeric picks are now post-launch tuning knobs: they feed T3.19 (tunables externalization + telemetry-grounded tuning), where real player data — not a static review — calibrates them. Kept here as the bookkeeping ledger so Brian sees the picks before they compound.

syn6_questions.decided_2026_06_14: WEEKLY CEREMONIAL (Brian): ATTUNE_COOLDOWN_SECS -> 7d AND cooldown key per-CHARACTER GLOBAL (not per-landmark). Build per design_calls_resolved_recent BRIAN_ROADMAP_DECISIONS.2026-06-14 decision 3. SHIPPED 2026-06-14 (drop kyber-weekly-ceremonial): 7d + attune_weekly per-char global key + QUALITY_FLOOR 65 (duds) + DC 15, in engine/kyber_attunement.py; tests/test_syn6c updated.
alternatives: 24h per-character GLOBAL (any landmark consumes the daily) — tighter; 1 kyber/Jedi/day across the map
Weekly per-character — ceremonial; 1 kyber/Jedi/week aligns with Padawan-trial narrative
24h per-landmark per-character (current) — 4 force-resonant landmarks in CW × 24h = 4 shards/Jedi/day max

No cooldown but per-landmark consumed-this-week tag (region quality model) — landmark goes quiet for the week after first attune by any character; preserves single-Jedi pacing but adds cross-Jedi competition
syn6_questions.supply_math: Current: 4 shards/Jedi/day theoretical max (need to visit 4 landmarks). Lightsaber needs 1 kyber. So one Jedi can craft 4 lightsabers/day or accumulate stacks indefinitely. Likely oversupplied for the one-and-done canonical need.
syn6_questions.concern: HIGH PRIORITY — most likely to be off. Per-landmark cooldown was the easy implementation choice; per-character (or per-week) is probably the right design choice for scarcity. Need a Brian call on whether kyber is 'common-for-Jedi' or 'ceremonial-for-Jedi'.

syn6_questions.decided_2026_06_14: WEEKLY CEREMONIAL (Brian): allow OCCASIONAL DUDS — lower QUALITY_FLOOR (~65) so marginal successes yield sub-q75 (unusable) shards; only good margins reach q75+. Build per BRIAN_ROADMAP_DECISIONS.2026-06-14 decision 3.
syn6_questions.value: q75 floor, q95 ceiling, +5Q per skill margin band
alternatives: Floor at q60, ceiling at q90 — broader band, some duds; matches existing crafting tier descriptors (Good=60+, Superior=80+, Masterwork=90+)
Floor at q75 (current, matches T5_MIN_QUALITY), ceiling q95 — every successful attune is T5-craftable; no duds
Floor at q70, ceiling q100, margin scaling — lets exceptional rolls cap at q100 but removes the SYN.8 reserved-tier semantics
Variable per-landmark (some landmarks 'minor', some 'major' kyber) — content-authoring layer
syn6_questions.supply_math: Current: every successful attune yields T5-craftable material. A skilled Jedi (8D Scholar) auto-passes DC 11 every time. No duds means every cooldown turns into supply.
syn6_questions.concern: No-duds design is generous. Pre-launch playtest should confirm: is the right shape 'always get something' (current) or 'sometimes get nothing'?

syn6_questions.decided_2026_06_14: WEEKLY CEREMONIAL (Brian): ATTUNE_DIFFICULTY 11 -> 15 (Difficult). Build per BRIAN_ROADMAP_DECISIONS.2026-06-14 decision 3.
syn6_questions.value: DC 11 (Moderate), Knowledge attribute or scholar/willpower skill
alternatives: DC 11 (Moderate, current) — typical Jedi clears easily
DC 15 (Difficult) — Padawan strains, Knight passes, Master auto-passes; tighter gate on quality outcomes
DC 21 (Very Difficult) — only Masters reliably succeed; cap matches T5 schematic-difficulty band; very ceremonial
syn6_questions.concern: Current DC 11 means a typical Jedi-faction starter character (3D Knowledge) succeeds ~50%, a trained one (4D+) succeeds nearly always. Pairs with the no-duds quality floor to give consistent yield.

alternatives: 30 min per-region (current) — design doc value
30 min global — prevents region-cycling farm; tighter session loop but penalizes legitimate roaming play
60 min per-region + 30 min global floor — caps both cycling and single-spot farming
Reset on death — combat-tied cooldown
syn6_questions.supply_math: Current: a player cycling through 5+ wilderness regions per session could harvest 5+ × yield/30min — accumulates credits + resources at session-length pace. No global supply ceiling.
syn6_questions.concern: 30min/region was the design number; per-region was the right axis. But the lack of a session-level ceiling means a determined harvester can fill inventory in one play session. Maybe acceptable; needs playtest.

alternatives: 10% — softer visitor friction
15% (current, design number)
20-25% — heavier owner advantage, more incentive to claim
Variable by region security (lawless 10%, contested 20%) — content-axis dial
syn6_questions.concern: 15% is the design number, no debate needed unless the economist pass surfaces it as a friction issue.

alternatives: 0.7×..1.3× (current, design number) — ±30% swing
0.8×..1.2× — tamer, less crafter-travel motivation
0.5×..1.5× — wider, stronger 'follow the news' pull
syn6_questions.concern: Design number; preserve unless economist pass calls for tightening.

syn6_questions.value: 6-row table per design §2.5.2 (Contested foothold 100-200cr → Lawless control 400-800cr)
alternatives: Design numbers (current)
Halved for soft-launch tuning
Doubled for 'cash-rich crafter' lane
syn6_questions.concern: Pairs with weekly variance + skill-margin scaling. End-to-end credit-per-hour-of-harvest math hasn't been computed. Economist pass should derive: typical Jedi-faction Knight playing 1h, harvesting 2 regions, gets X credits. Then compare against vendor prices, weapon costs, ship maintenance, etc.

syn6_questions.decided_2026_06_14: CAP at +100% (Brian): cap bands at 5 so the credit multiplier maxes at 2.0x (was uncapped). Build per BRIAN_ROADMAP_DECISIONS.2026-06-14 decision 4.
syn6_questions.value: +20% credits per 5-pt margin band, no cap
alternatives: +20% per band uncapped (current) — a 50-margin roll gives 11× the base credits
+20% per band capped at 5 bands (+100%) — exceptional rolls double the base, no more
+10% per band, capped at +50% — softer reward curve
syn6_questions.concern: Current uncapped scaling means a Master-tier harvester can get extreme credit outliers. Tests use 12D Survival which produces consistent +large margins. Real play might be tamer, but a 10D+ Survival skill is achievable post-progression.

alternatives: 10% (current) — rare but reachable; +1 rare stack at q100 per ~10 control-tier harvests
5% — half as common; aligns with the 'reserved for SYN.8 Tier-3 anomaly drops' framing of q100
20% — twice as common; tempts more Control-tier-region traffic
syn6_questions.concern: Today this is the only path to q100 'rare' stacks (the post-launch T5 recipes won't use 'rare' as a primary mat anyway). Will become moot when SYN.8 lands and q100 'rare' is design-reserved for anomaly drops — at that point reduce or remove.

alternatives: 5% — half as common; closer to peer T5 scarcity
7% (current) — ~1 per ~7h solo harvesting
10% — matches generic t5_rare; risks Coruscant becoming the path of least resistance for the whole T5 lane
syn6_questions.concern: Only faucet for scavenged_republic_tech; sole sink is t5_hyperdrive_surge_converter (12,000cr). Solo-harvestable, deliberately easier than the participation-gated peer T5 mats per the non-PvP-crafter design (§2.5.6).

alternatives: 25-28 (current) — Master crafter with 8D+ skill + Wild Die explosion typical
30-32 (Heroic) — even Masters need exceptional rolls; pairs better with 'genuine endgame' framing
20-23 (Difficult top) — overlaps existing T4 ceiling; might dilute the T5 boundary
syn6_questions.concern: T1-T4 ceiling is 20. T5 at 25-28 is clearly above. Pre-launch playtest with a Master crafter should confirm success rates feel right (target: 50-70% success at intended progression tier).

syn6_questions.value: T5 mat q75+, standard T1-T4 components at q50-65
alternatives: Current: q75 T5 mat + q50-65 standard mats
Tighter: q75 T5 mat + q65-75 standard mats — pulls in more standard-material economy
Looser: q75 T5 mat + q40-50 standard mats — only the T5 mat matters; standard mats are filler
syn6_questions.concern: Current bands mean a Master crafter who can't source q50+ standard materials is locked out even with T5 mats. Pairs with weekly variance (which can push a region's metal to 0.7× → harder to find q50+ stacks). Real bottleneck question is whether the standard-mat floor is the right shape.

alternatives: 1 per craft (current) — minimum scarcity
2-3 per craft for more potent items (e.g. master-grade armor) — differentiates T5 outputs by gating cost
Variable by output_type — lightsaber 1, ship part 2, armor 1
syn6_questions.concern: Flat 1-per-craft means every T5 output costs the same in T5-mat terms. Differentiation could come from T1-T4 component costs (current shape) or from T5-mat costs.

syn6_questions.value: Region-scoped fallback — no flags in region → every room qualifies
alternatives: Region-scoped fallback (current) — incremental authoring; un-audited regions stay open
Global fallback — only triggers if NO room anywhere has the flag
Hard gate, no fallback — all rooms require the flag from day one
syn6_questions.concern: Current allows content team to migrate one region at a time without breaking unmarked regions. Fallback path goes dormant once all regions are flagged (post-launch content pass). Acceptable as a transitional design; economist pass should confirm long-term shape after content pass completes.

tunable_open_questions.syn7_questions_pending: Will be appended as SYN.7.b drops land — Tier 2 anomaly reward shape (15-25 influence is the design number, but the per-tier loot tables, multi-participant distribution rules, and corvette-boarding-ground-adaptation specifics all need explicit picks).

syn7a_questions.value: hourly tick + 0.4 spawn-chance → expected ~2.5h average interval
alternatives: hourly tick + 0.4 chance (current) → matches design '2-3h per region'
hourly tick + 0.5 chance → 2h avg (faster, more visible activity for small playerbase)
hourly tick + 0.33 chance → 3h avg (slower, more scarce)
30-min tick + variable chance — finer control but tighter polling load
syn7a_questions.supply_math: With 2 wilderness regions (Tatooine Dune Sea, Coruscant Underworld) and 0.4 spawn-chance per region per hour, expected ~0.8 anomaly spawns/hour across the world. Per-region cap MAX_PER_REGION=2 means a single region tops out at 2 concurrent. For a small playerbase that's about right; for a quiet server with 1-2 active players a 2.5h cadence means a player playing 1 hour rarely sees a fresh spawn during their session.
syn7a_questions.concern: Design number aligned; needs playtest to confirm 'feels right' density. Spawn-chance 0.4 was picked to hit the design's 2-3h midpoint; legitimate tunable. Halving the chance (0.2 → 5h avg) might better match a sparse server.

alternatives: 30 min (current, design number)
60 min — gives players a wider response window
15 min — tighter urgency, harder to catch
syn7a_questions.concern: Design number. Preserve unless playtest shows players consistently arrive too late.

alternatives: 2 (current) — prevents accumulation during inactive periods
1 — strict pacing, one-at-a-time
3-5 — allows backlog for quiet periods
syn7a_questions.concern: 2 is a reasonable cap. With ~30 min duration and ~2.5h cadence, a region naturally hovers near 0-1 anomalies most of the time. Cap mostly relevant for the early-game cold-start window when no one's resolving.

alternatives: +5 (current, design number)
+3-7 variable by template difficulty — finer-grained pacing
+2-3 — even tighter; combined with high cadence this nudges toward 'lots of small wins' play
syn7a_questions.concern: Design number. The supply math depends on resolution rate: a faction with one active player resolving every anomaly in their territory gets +5 inf × N anomalies × resolve rate. At ~2.5h/region cadence and ~2 regions in active use, that's +5 × 8/day × 0.5 resolve rate × 2 regions = ~40 inf/day per region per active faction. Foothold (50) reached in ~6 days, Dominant (200) in ~5 weeks. Pre-launch playtest should confirm this curve is right for the intended progression.

alternatives: DC 13 (current) — Moderate-Difficult; starter 3D character rolls average 10-11 → fails most; trained 4D-5D rolls 14-17 → succeeds most
DC 11 — Moderate; even starter chars succeed roughly half the time
DC 15 — Difficult; tightly gated, only trained characters reliably succeed
syn7a_questions.concern: DC 13 is meant to be 'newer players sometimes fail, trained players reliably succeed.' Pairs with one-shot semantics (failed resolution still consumes the anomaly + grants reduced reward). Playtest target: ~70% success rate for the intended-tier player (4D in the relevant skill).

syn7a_questions.value: Per-template credit bands (100-500cr range) + 1-4 resource stacks at q30-60
alternatives: Current bands (100-500cr success, 0-100cr fail; 1-4 stacks at q30-60)
Wider success-vs-fail gap — make success much more rewarding, fail nearly nothing
Higher quality on resources — q50-70 instead of q30-60 — feeds T5 crafting more aggressively
Lower credit, higher resource — emphasizes the crafter-pipeline aspect
syn7a_questions.concern: Picked to feel proportionate to the time/risk: ~30 min effort, skill check vs DC 13, +5 influence. Compare against harvest yields (100-800cr per harvest with 30-min regional cooldown) — anomalies should sit in the same band as harvest but with the influence kicker as the differentiator. End-to-end credit-per-hour math hasn't been computed; defer to economist pass.

syn7a_questions.value: Failed skill check still grants partial reward (anomaly consumed)
alternatives: Current: one-shot, fail consumes anomaly + partial reward (anti-farm + low frustration)
Fail leaves anomaly active for others — encourages handoff but lets first-arriver retry
Fail consumes anomaly + no reward — punishing; might feel bad
No skill check, just walk-up resolution — drops the resolution mechanic entirely
syn7a_questions.concern: One-shot semantics prevents farming and prevents player A failing then player B succeeding (which would feel arbitrary). Partial reward on fail keeps players engaged — failing still feels worthwhile. Tusken party fail_reward is 0/0/0 because narratively losing a combat shouldn't pay out; that's an intentional asymmetry within the catalog.

alternatives: Uniform (current) — every template equally likely
Region-flavored weighting — Tusken party more likely in Dune Sea, CIS probe more likely in CIS-traffic regions
Faction-state weighting — Director sees active CW conflict in region → CIS probe more likely
Rarity-weighted — common templates 30%, rare 10%
syn7a_questions.concern: Uniform is the simple shape. Region-flavored weighting would be a SYN.7.b polish drop and ties into the Director AI's existing event-weighting mechanics. Defer until Tier 2 templates land so region-specific weighting can be designed once across both tiers.

tunable_open_questions.syn8_questions_pending: Will be appended as SYN.8 drops — Tier 3 world boss numbers (HP, multi-phase HP thresholds, participation scaling, kyber-pearl drop count formula floor(participants/4) — design number, but the participant denominator could be tighter or looser).
tunable_open_questions.syn9_questions_pending: Will be appended as SYN.9 drops — building construction costs, category effect magnitudes, construction time, demolish refund.
