tier_3_post_launch.title: Pre-launch tunables externalization + telemetry instrumentation (knob config files + post-launch tuning loop)
tier_3_post_launch.timing: NEAR-LAUNCH refactor (the externalization), then POST-LAUNCH ongoing (the tuning loop). Brian's framing 2026-06-06: 'near the end of development, before we go live.'
tier_3_post_launch.goal: Two halves. (1) EXTERNALIZE every tuning lever (the magic numbers currently living as module-level constants / inline literals across the engine) into versioned configuration files, so a knob can be tweaked without a code edit or a redeploy-to-change-a-number. (2) Add ROBUST, STRUCTURED logging/telemetry at the decision points those levers govern, so after launch Claude can read the telemetry and recommend knob changes grounded in real player behavior.
tier_3_post_launch.knob_domains_to_sweep: Non-exhaustive starting catalog (grep these out of the modules and lift to config): ECONOMY — adjust_credits faucet/sink rates, P2P_DAILY_CAP, trade SUPPLY_POOL/DEMAND_POOL sizes + refresh timers + volume_premium curve, FINE_FRACTION / smuggling EV, vendor salvage-price scaling + listing TTL, sink prices (home-prestige/vanity-title/commissary tiers, spacedock fee), gear-insurance premium, city tax rates. DIFFICULTY — security-zone combat gates, customs Perception/Con difficulties + infraction fines, wilderness encounter weights + creature spawn counts, hazard-table thresholds, NPC archetype hostility, space astrogation/scan difficulties. PROGRESSION — CP award rates + milestone thresholds (ships_log + the milestone-CP meter), chain rewards, faction-rep tier breakpoints + shop modifiers, Padawan/Master bond pacing. FORCE — DSP award/decay rates, mind-influence opposed-roll baselines, the DSP-bounty trigger threshold. DIRECTOR — alert-level thresholds, world-event fire probabilities + durations + dormant-effect multipliers (bounty_reward_mult, pirate/patrol_spawn_mult, etc.), milestone faction-average thresholds. SPAWN/POPULATION — NPC counts, traffic-ship density, idle-queue cadence. PERSISTENT-THREAT KNOBS (flagged by Brian 2026-06-06 as a concrete example — 'hunter aggression is one of the variables I'd want a config to tune: how strong are they? how often will they pursue a given PC?'): the roaming DSP bounty hunter's AGGRESSION — per-DSP-tier combat strength (engine/dsp_hunter.py hunter_combat_sheet, blaster 5D/6D/7D), pursuit CADENCE (step_for_dsp 5/9/14, advance_progress clamp 0..100, the dsp_hunter_tick interval=120/offset=75, the at_heels live-spawn gate), and the wanted threshold (bounty_board.DSP_BOUNTY_THRESHOLD=4); plus the cult COMMUNAL OBJECTIVE knobs (engine/communal_objective.py MENACE_START 35 / MENACE_PER_MINUTE 0.35 / DEADLINE_HOURS 48 / STRIKE_COOLDOWN_S 600 / strike difficulty 10/13/16 / reduction bands 5/8/11/14 / REP_FLOOR 3..REP_MAX 15 / TITLE_SHARE_THRESHOLD 0.10 / the runtime REPOST_COOLDOWN 6h) and the smuggling checkpoint scrutiny (engine/smuggling.py CHECKPOINT_PATROL_BOOST 0.15 + CHECKPOINT_DIFFICULTY_BOOST 3, already named constants, alongside the still-inline lockdown 0.30/+5).
tier_3_post_launch.approach_notes: Phase it, no big-bang. (a) Inventory pass: grep the tuning literals per domain; pick a format — YAML fits the existing content pipeline (one file per domain: economy_tuning.yaml / difficulty_tuning.yaml / progression_tuning.yaml / force_tuning.yaml / director_tuning.yaml, or a tuning/ dir). (b) Loader with SAFE DEFAULTS: every knob keeps its current in-code value as the default so a missing/partial config can never brick the game (fail-safe, like the era/lore loaders). (c) Hot-reload is nice-to-have, not required for v1 (restart-to-apply is fine for tuning). (d) Telemetry: structured (JSON-line) events at each governed decision — emit inputs + the rolled/derived output + the knob values in effect, so a recommendation can say 'at knob X=N the outcome distribution was Y.' Reuse the existing logging infra; add a dedicated telemetry sink. (e) Guard tests: config round-trips; a missing config falls back to in-code defaults; telemetry emitters fire with the expected schema.
tier_3_post_launch.ties_into: Absorbs the ad-hoc TUNABLE notes scattered through _notes (gear-insurance premium, prestige steepness, vanity costs, commissary, FINE_FRACTION, P2P cap, vendor TTL) and the tunable_open_questions block — those become entries in the new config files. Natural home for the Director AI tuning knobs (T3.15).
tier_3_post_launch.blocks_or_after: AFTER feature-complete — this is a hardening/operability pass, not new gameplay. Do NOT start mid-feature: it should sweep a SETTLED codebase so the knob inventory is stable. The telemetry half keeps paying off for the life of the live game.
tier_3_post_launch.telemetry_purpose_clarified: Brian 2026-06-06: the telemetry half exists to collect data USEFUL TO CLAUDE for post-launch analysis -> recommend config-knob changes (the T3.19 levers) to keep the game on track / more FUN (Brian's explicit, admittedly-vague goal). Capture broadly now; Claude analyzes dumps offline post-launch. Implementation posture: append-only JSON-line events to a dedicated sink, high-frequency events SAMPLED, all emitters async/buffered/non-blocking (T3.21 guards this) -- NEVER slow the game loop. Aggregation is offline, not in-process.

telemetry_metrics_catalog.A_engagement_and_fun_proxies: Session-length distribution; sessions/player/week; D1/D7/D30 return rate; time-of-day concurrency (a MUSH needs critical-mass population to be fun); time-from-login-to-first-meaningful-action (onboarding friction); and the LAST ACTION before a player stops returning (a 'fun-cliff' detector -- what were they doing when the game lost them).
telemetry_metrics_catalog.B_pacing_progression_economy: CP earn-rate vs spend-rate + time-to-milestone distributions (detect grind or stall); credit-balance distribution + wealth Gini over time (inflation/inequality, the economist's worry now measurable); PER-FAUCET inflow + PER-SINK outflow rates (which faucets dominate, which sinks are vestigial -- directly maps to a config knob); skill/attribute advancement curves + how long players sit at each power tier.
telemetry_metrics_catalog.C_content_alive_utilization: Command-frequency histogram (a dead feature shows up as ~0 use); per-SYSTEM engagement split (combat / crafting / smuggling / sabacc / Force / social / exploration -- is the cross-playstyle design actually working or does one loop dominate?); room/zone/world visit heatmap (find the ghost towns); mission/quest started->completed->abandoned funnels (abandonment = friction or boredom); cult communal-objective participation rate + win/lose ratio + contribution spread; DSP-hunter spawn -> beaten vs collected.
telemetry_metrics_catalog.D_friction_and_frustration: Combat win/loss/flee rates by encounter type; PC-death frequency + CAUSE (creature/PvP/hazard/hunter); skill-check success rates by difficulty band (are DCs calibrated -- too-easy is boring, too-hard frustrates); a 'STUCK' signal = the same failed action repeated N times (a wall players can't pass -- quest/tutorial friction); command-error / 'huh?' rate per command (UX friction, which verbs confuse); a rage-quit proxy = logoff immediately after a death/loss/failure.
telemetry_metrics_catalog.E_social_fabric_MUSH: For a MUSH, social IS the fun. say/pose/emote/channel volume; multi-player interaction density + social-graph breadth (distinct partners per player); solo vs grouped activity ratio; NEW-PLAYER social integration (does a newcomer get talked to / grouped within session 1 -- one of the biggest MUSH retention drivers); entertainer/audience-system usage; RP-scene length/frequency.
telemetry_metrics_catalog.F_operational_perf_and_noslowdown_guard: Per-command latency p50/p95/p99; tick duration; DB-query times; SPA wire round-trip; error/exception rate per subsystem. Doubles as the perf-regression detector AND the guardrail that the telemetry itself + the game stay fast (the no-slowdown caveat, enforced under T3.21).
telemetry_metrics_catalog.STANDOUT_outside_the_box_fun_proxies: (1) VALENCE DISAMBIGUATION via the follow-on action -- a death/failure followed by IMMEDIATE RETRY reads as challenge-fun ('I'll get it this time'); the SAME death followed by LOGOFF reads as frustration. Identical event, opposite valence; the next action tells you which. Probably the single most useful fun-vs-frustration signal. (2) VOLUNTARY REPETITION -- a player re-running a loop (smuggling run, hunt, sabacc hand) UNPROMPTED with no quest forcing it = that loop is fun. (3) 'ONE MORE TURN' -- activity in the final minutes before logoff / continuing past a natural stopping point = the compulsion loop is working. (4) LOGIN-TIMING AROUND EVENTS -- do players log in MORE around scheduled cult uprisings / world events? Anticipation = the events are a genuine draw. (5) INTRINSIC GOALS -- time on activities with NO mechanical reward (pure RP, exploration, base decoration, helping newbies). Intrinsic motivation is the deepest fun signal there is. (6) CHAT-AFFECT SPIKES -- clustering of exclamations / caps / laughter-emotes / rapid poses around an event = a crude 'something exciting just happened' detector; correlate the spike with its trigger to locate the fun MOMENTS. (7) VARIETY-SEEKING vs ROTE-GRIND -- a player trying NEW systems/commands over time = curiosity/engagement; a narrow loop forever is ambiguous (fun-grind or boredom) -- disambiguate with the session-length TREND (lengthening = fun, shortening = burning out). (8) SOCIAL CONTAGION -- does one player's act (starting a brawl, posting a bounty, rallying the cult) pull OTHERS into the same activity within a window? High contagion = the feature creates emergent, shared fun.

tier_3_post_launch.BEFORE_IMPLEMENTING_REVIEW: Brian 2026-06-06: the telemetry_metrics_catalog above is a FIRST-DRAFT brainstorm, NOT a build spec. Do NOT blindly implement it as written. Claude must RE-REVIEW and EXPAND the metric list at implementation time, with ECONOMY depth as the explicit gap (Brian's focus area; the current bucket B is thin for an economy-heavy game). Economy directions to flesh out then: per-good price stability + supply/demand pool levels over time, trade volume by route, realized smuggling EV vs theoretical, per-SINK engagement (which sinks actually drain credits vs are vestigial), per-FAUCET share, sabacc/gambling flows, NPC-buyback usage, P2P transfer patterns (also RMT/exploit detection), wealth Gini trend. More broadly: this 're-think before building, don't blindly implement the TODO' discipline applies to ALL design-heavy TODO items, not just telemetry.

tier_3_post_launch.title: State-preservation / safe-migration robustness pass
tier_3_post_launch.timing: PRE-LAUNCH hardening, clustered with T3.19 (Brian 2026-06-06: 'right before or after we deal with telemetry and config'). After features are stable.
tier_3_post_launch.goal: Make the codebase robust so that ANY post-launch schema change or invasive change has a CLEAR, TESTED path that PRESERVES all players' live game state -- no data loss, no silently-dropped fields on upgrade. This is the safety net for the unavoidable post-launch changes; it's the direct reason Brian wants nearly everything implemented pre-launch (to minimize how often this net is needed).
tier_3_post_launch.scope_notes: (a) Migration-framework audit: migrations forward-only, idempotent, versioned, and each one REVERSIBLE-or-documented. (b) A migration test harness that runs every migration against a POPULATED/seeded DB (real characters, orgs, ships, inventories) -- not just a fresh DB -- and asserts no row/field loss; this is what catches the dangerous ones. (c) Defensive (de)serialization discipline: from_db_dict already tolerates partial/pre-migration rows; codify that as a contract and extend it everywhere. F.7.n-class bugs (DERIVED state that doesn't survive reload) live here -- a reload-round-trip invariant test per persisted entity. (d) Backup/restore + a documented upgrade runbook. (e) A DB-integrity validator that can scan a live DB for corruption/orphans before & after a migration. (f) A written 'state-preservation contract' that every future schema PR must honor (candidate arch invariant at the v52 consolidation).
tier_3_post_launch.ties_into: F.7.n (a Claude-FLAGGED, UNCONFIRMED force_sensitive-on-reload concern that Brian has NEVER observed -- VERIFY it here via a reload-round-trip test; CLOSE as not-a-bug if FS survives, rather than 'fixing' a bug that may not exist); the arch-doc v52 reconciliation (add the state-preservation invariant); db/database.py SCHEMA_VERSION + MIGRATIONS.
tier_3_post_launch.progress_2026_06_14: COMPLETE 2026-06-14 (scope_notes a-f all done; started drop migration-framework-harness). Shipped the migration-framework SAFETY guard (scope_notes a/b, realistic slice): tests/test_migration_framework_integrity.py asserts SCHEMA_VERSION==max(MIGRATIONS) (the silent forgot-to-bump drift bug), no orphaned migrations, valid statement lists, AND boot re-entrancy/data-preservation on a real temp-file Database (a populated reboot is a no-op: seeded account+character survive, version stays 44, no duplicate schema_version row). DESIGN NUANCE recorded: scope_notes(b)'s 'run every migration against a populated DB' is the WRONG test -- the 8 data-backfill statements in MIGRATIONS (mail_v33 INSERT etc.) are intentionally once-only behind the version gate, so re-applying them would falsely flag idempotency; the realistic property is the reboot no-op. CLOSED a false launch concern: the readiness-review 'additive fields shipped on schema 43 w/o a bump need a save backfill' (chain kind / questline slots / threat_band / min_band/max_band) is NOT a bug -- all are config/world-YAML/definition fields read with explicit defaults, regenerated each load, never persisted per-save; real column adds DO migrate via ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN DEFAULT (30->44). scope_notes(c) STARTED (drop character-reload-roundtrip): tests/test_character_reload_roundtrip.py locks the Character to_db_dict<->from_db_dict CORE-field contract (every core field survives reload incl. force_sensitive/attrs/skills/equipment-keys; to_db_dict stable under round-trip) -- the most important persisted entity. F.7.n (force_sensitive reload) ALREADY COVERED by test_f7n_force_attribute_seeding.py::TestReloadReconstructsForceSensitive -- close as not-a-bug. scope_notes(c) EXTENDED (drop persisted-entity-roundtrip): tests/test_persisted_entity_roundtrip.py locks the reload-round-trip for the five board/inventory/buff entities that persist via a to_dict/from_dict pair (ItemInstance, Mission, BountyContract, SmugglingJob, Buff) -- pure dict contract + the REAL json.dumps/json.loads production path + to_dict-stability + enum-TYPE survival (isinstance, not just scalar); all five contracts audited CLEAN (no save-data-loss); pinned ItemInstance default-omission + Buff empty-display_name normalization, and closed the test_bounty_board_unit chain_bounty_id coverage gap. 25 tests. scope_notes(e) DONE (drop db-integrity-validator): db/integrity.py::scan_integrity wraps PRAGMA integrity_check + foreign_key_check (covers all 80+ declared REFERENCES FKs, no per-table SQL) into a structured IntegrityReport (.ok/.summary); operator CLI tools/check_db_integrity.py runs it before/after a migration + on a restored backup (exit 0 clean / 1 findings / 2 usage). Read-only. Confirmed a fresh seeded DB is referentially CLEAN + a parent-dropped-with-FK-off orphan (characters->accounts) is detected. NOTE framing: foreign_keys=ON at connect already blocks orphan WRITES on the live path, so this is the belt-and-suspenders scan for the enforcement-bypass paths (table-rebuild migration toggles FK off / backup-restore / manual edit). 6 tests. scope_notes(c) COMPLETE for all deserializer pairs (drop serializer-roundtrip-extra added TrafficShip to_json/from_json + NPCConfig to_dict/from_dict, tests/test_serializer_roundtrip_extra.py, 9 tests): an AST scan finds exactly 8 from_dict/from_db_dict/from_json deserializers (Character, ItemInstance, Mission, BountyContract, SmugglingJob, Buff, TrafficShip, NPCConfig) and every one now has a reload-round-trip guard. NOTE orgs/ships/questline-state persist via direct DB COLUMNS (no dataclass serializer), so they have no from_dict to round-trip; a DB-row-level round-trip for them would be a separate, lower-priority slice. scope_notes(d) DONE (drop db-backup-restore): db/backup.py::backup_database does a CONSISTENT online snapshot via SQLite's backup API (safe on a live DB); operator CLI tools/backup_db.py [--overwrite] [--verify] (--verify runs scan_integrity on the snapshot); docs/design/backup_restore_runbook_v1.md documents the backup->scan->migrate->scan loop + the stop-server/file-replace restore. Composes with scope_notes(e). 5 tests + CLI smoke. scope_notes(f) DONE (drop state-preservation-contract): docs/design/state_preservation_contract_v1.md codifies the 5 invariants (I1 reload-round-trip per persisted entity / I2 derived state reconstructs / I3 forward-only migrations + SCHEMA_VERSION drift guard / I4 no orphans+corruption / I5 backup-before-migrate) + a PR checklist; tests/test_state_preservation_contract.py makes it SELF-ENFORCING -- a static AST scan of engine/ai/db/server/parser asserts the set of from_dict/from_db_dict/from_json deserializers == a registry of round-trip-tested entities (a new serializer can't ship without a round-trip guard), plus serializer-pair symmetry, registered-test-files-exist, and no phantom artifact pointers in the doc. 5 tests. **T3.20 CLUSTER COMPLETE: scope_notes a/b/c/d/e/f all DONE; F.7.n closed as not-a-bug. The candidate arch invariant (I1-I5) is owed a fold into the v52 consolidation (arch-doc lane).**

tier_3_post_launch.title: Full optimization + security pass
tier_3_post_launch.timing: PRE-LAUNCH hardening, after features are stable (optimize a SETTLED codebase) and after T3.19 (so it can also verify telemetry adds no latency). Brian 2026-06-06.
tier_3_post_launch.goal: Two halves. OPTIMIZATION: profile and tighten hot paths so the live game stays fast under load (and so the T3.19 telemetry is provably non-blocking -- the no-slowdown caveat). SECURITY: harden the full attack surface before exposing the game to the public internet.
tier_3_post_launch.scope_notes: OPTIMIZATION -- profile tick handlers, the combat resolver, the SPA wire protocol, and DB access (indexes, N+1 queries, aiosqlite patterns, connection reuse); make every telemetry emitter async/buffered/sampled; watch hot-path allocation + memory growth over a long session. SECURITY -- input validation across ALL command parsers + the web API; SQL-parameterization audit (mostly done via the ledger work -- verify globally); auth/session/token handling; rate-limiting beyond P2P_DAILY_CAP (per-command + per-API); the aiohttp surface (CORS, headers, request-size/DoS limits); secrets handling; an AccessLevel-enforcement audit on every admin/Director command; the web-client XSS/injection surface (the SPA renders server strings).
tier_3_post_launch.ties_into: T3.19 telemetry (must be non-blocking); the web_client protocol; AccessLevel discipline; the existing P2P rate-limit as the rate-limiting precedent.

tier_3_post_launch.title: Ambient NPC life (idle-Ollama background world simulation)
tier_3_post_launch.timing: DESIGN done now. Phase 0 (DB scaffolding) DELIVERED pre-launch drop 46; (empty CREATE TABLE migration + accessor stubs, inert) so the post-launch build never migrates a live populated DB. Phases 1-4 (the sim + idle-Ollama flavor) are POST-LAUNCH.
tier_3_post_launch.goal: Make the world feel alive: NPCs have goals, move through the environment, and interact with EACH OTHER as background flavor, driven by the existing Ollama idle queue at a priority BELOW all PC-facing work. NPCs are observable, never targeting PCs (no unprompted PC interaction in v1, per Brian). Python-first: the sim (goals/movement/interaction) is deterministic Python on the tick; Ollama only flavors interactions the sim already decided, and is fully preemptible (degrades to templated strings when busy).
tier_3_post_launch.extend_not_add: Verified against HEAD: engine/idle_queue.py already has the priority+backoff+preemption model (AmbientBarkTask precedent) -> add AmbientLifeTask below all existing tasks. server/tick_scheduler.py -> register one ambient_npc_life_tick sibling. engine/npc_space_traffic.py is the movement pattern to MIRROR for ground NPCs (room_id transitions). Sits BESIDE engine/world_events.py (macro drama vs micro churn). No new top-level system.
tier_3_post_launch.db_design: Reuse npcs.ai_config_json for config (ambient_enabled default FALSE = every existing NPC unchanged until opted in). ONE new table npc_ambient_state (+ optional npc_ambient_relationship), each with a JSON `extra TEXT DEFAULT '{}'` column = the SQLite-idiomatic future-proof 'blank' (Brian's telemetry-style instinct; NOT reserved typed columns). GENERAL POLICY adopted: every new table gets an `extra` JSON column at creation.
tier_3_post_launch.live_safety: ambient_enabled defaults false (zero behavior change until opt-in); sim NEVER calls Ollama on the tick path (world cadence independent of Ollama load); AmbientLifeTask below all idle tasks + PC talk bypasses the queue (can never delay a player); ambient acts have NO mechanical effects in v1 (no market/combat/faction state change -> faucet/sink-neutral); Phase-0 DB scaffolding is empty CREATE TABLE landed pre-launch (lowest-risk migration class), so the post-launch sim is pure feature code.
tier_3_post_launch.ties_into: T3.20 (state-preservation/safe-migration -- Phase 0 IS a safe-migration exemplar); T3.19 (telemetry -- ambient ticks should emit the same structured telemetry); T3.15 (Director AI -- optional future read-coupling: world events / Director mood could nudge ambient goals).
tier_3_post_launch.open_calls: Logged in the design doc §8 for when this comes off the shelf: (1) intra-zone vs cross-zone movement (rec intra-zone v1); (2) ambient-NPC population cap for tick budget; (3) observability surface (room channel vs ambient channel vs look-only); (4) the PC-interaction boundary (OUT for v1, revisit after).
tier_3_post_launch.phase_0_status: DELIVERED 2026-06-13 (drop 46): schema v44 (npc_ambient_state + npc_ambient_relationship, both in SCHEMA_SQL + MIGRATIONS[44], idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, each with a JSON `extra` blank) + inert accessor stubs (ambient_state_get/ensure_row/update/in_room, mirror cp_ticks, NO callers yet) + tests/test_t3_22_ambient_life_phase0.py (13). Invariant-auditor CLEAN, smoke BOOTS CLEAN. Phases 1-4 (the sim + idle-Ollama flavor) remain POST-LAUNCH.

tier_3_post_launch.title: Party skill challenges (D&D-style skill-diverse team content)
tier_3_post_launch.effort: medium (engine seam small; content authoring open-ended)
tier_3_post_launch.timing: POST-LAUNCH. PRE-LAUNCH SEAM SHIPPED 2026-06-16 (Sonnet loop, drop t3-23-skill-gate-phase0): WildernessAnomaly.phase_skill_gate() accessor + _advance_to_next_phase inert-seam branch landed; 16 tests. Field is INERT -- Phase 1 build wires skill-check resolution post-launch.
tier_3_post_launch.goal: End-game content that rewards a skill-diverse TEAM (rogue=locks, face=social, cleric=medicine, wizard=Force, demolitions=breach, fighters) the way a D&D party does. A multi-phase encounter where each phase gates a DIFFERENT skill, so no single character has all the end-game-depth skills to clear it efficiently.
tier_3_post_launch.brian_calls: (1) SOFT-REQUIRE: soloable but punishing (a solo_penalty raises gate difficulty for a lone wolf; NO hard min-party-size lock in v1 -> no dead content at low pop). (2) Rewards = PARTICIPATION-SCALED t5 mats + credits (reuse the T3 anomaly reward model; feeds the t5 master-trainer crafting economy from drops 33-35). (3) Full design doc written now, build post-launch.
tier_3_post_launch.extend_not_add: EXTENDS engine/wilderness_anomalies.py: the multi-phase + participation (kill_counts) + skill-check (investigate) + participation-scaled-reward (_payout_combat_anomaly) substrate ALL exist. New: an additive per-phase `skill_gate` field (skill/difficulty/alt_skills/solo_penalty), a contribution_log extending participation to non-combat clears. Existing combat-only anomalies unchanged (no skill_gate = today's behavior). No new top-level system, command, schema migration, or reward faucet. All role skills already exist as live verbs (breach, picklock, stim, Force, social, combat).
tier_3_post_launch.open_calls: Design doc s8: solo_penalty magnitude, failure cost (rec retry-allowed + small resource cost), alt_skills breadth (rec 1-2), first-challenge location/theme (rec Coruscant Underworld vault), and whether a unique gated drop is added later vs v1's t5-mats.

tier_3_post_launch.title: Generalized quest/scenario expansion (beyond tutorial chains + T5 questlines)
tier_3_post_launch.launch_ruling: POST-LAUNCH body (Brian 2026-06-13 — the explicit exception to launch-is-whole-backlog), CONDITIONED on a painless path that is now VERIFIED at code level: the chain engine (engine/tutorial_chains.py + chain_events.py) is already the generalization target (kind: questline + active_questline slot); per-character quest STATE rides the character `attributes` JSON blob (NO live-DB migration); all 4 reward funnels + 11 chain events already exist. PRE-LAUNCH obligation: essentially none blocking (optional: reserve a quest_progress JSON column; keep questline state JSON-based, which it is). Content bridge: a `--target hooks` mode on tools/ingest_lore.py turns sourcebook adventure hooks into mission/bounty seeds pre-launch. Post-launch build = widen slots 2->N + add completion/trigger types (each additive, emit seam in the same drop) + author chains.yaml questlines.
tier_3_post_launch.first_slice_delivered: 2026-06-22 (drop generalized-questline-ghost-shipment, Opus loop): the AUTHOR-MORE-QUESTLINES build step, first slice. 'The Ghost Shipment' (nar_freight_ghost_shipment) -- the first ACCESSIBLE (non-t5, non-tutorial) kind:questline chain, reusing the live engine with NO new engine code/slot/event type. Accessible freelance side-content (prerequisites chargen_complete, no rep gate, ordinary Nar Shaddaa city rooms) -- proves the generalized-quest path beyond the rep-gated t5 trainer economy. 5 steps (talk/search/streetwise/combat_won/talk) reusing existing event types; 2 NPCs (Yelza Korrin giver + Brakkis Vone chain-tagged antagonist); rewards via the existing funnels (300cr + 5 independent rep + ghost_shipment_solved achievement cp_reward 3); tests/test_generalized_questline_ghost_shipment.py + the generic TestAllQuestlinesWalkable. Remaining post-launch widening still open: slots 2->N (genuine design call -- how many concurrent questlines + UX), new completion/trigger types (bounty-completion, skill-up, compound objectives -- each needs its consumer in the same drop), and the tools/ingest_lore.py --target hooks content bridge. More accessible questlines can be authored incrementally on this same pattern.

design_calls_pending_brian.title: Rework communal events (rally/cults) from a menace COUNTER into staged playable SCENARIOS — the whole system, not just Hollow Sun
design_calls_pending_brian.origin: Brian 2026-06-21 played Cult of the Hollow Sun: 'typing rally / rally strike isn't gameplay'. Events should be: go to a location, cooperate, waves of enemies / locations to traverse, varied skills (slice terminals).
design_calls_pending_brian.current_state: engine/communal_objective.py CULT_ROSTER (all cults mechanically identical) + parser/communal_commands.py rally/'rally strike' = a global menace meter chipped by one auto-rolled best-pool strike per ~10 min. Explicitly an MVP ('design III.3'; 'rally-objective mechanics are design-open').
design_calls_pending_brian.plan: Compose existing primitives — wilderness-anomaly multi-phase WAVE combat + skill-mode (perform_skill_check) + the tutorial-chain/questline step engine (T3.24) — into multi-stage SITE scenarios: travel -> wave combat -> multi-skill objectives (slice conduits / free captives / break wards) -> boss -> resolve; cooperative; 'rally' becomes the find/track surface, not the gameplay. Extend, don't add. Vertical-slice Hollow Sun first, tune with Brian, then generalize to the roster.
design_calls_pending_brian.decision_needed: (1) TIMING — pre-launch (lean yes for at least the vertical slice; an unfun headline event is a launch-quality issue) vs right-of-launch? (2) FIRST SCENARIO SHAPE — which skills feature, how many waves, and solo-viable vs requires a small group (the multi-phase combat assumes 3-5 coordinating players)?

design_calls_pending_brian.title: BLOCKER: choosing Force-Sensitive at web chargen -> player logs in unable to use any Force power; how to represent force-sensitivity is balance/schema-touching
design_calls_pending_brian.origin: QA break-it sweep #2, 2026-06-22, confirmed end-to-end in-harness (BLOCKER + a HIGH + 2 MEDIUM all share this root).
design_calls_pending_brian.root_cause: Producer (server/api.py handle_submit + handle_create_character) stores a Force-sensitive char's control/sense/alter as 0D (the sentinel). Consumer (engine/character.py from_db_dict ~882) derives force_sensitive only from a NON-ZERO Force pool (the L2 fix test_qa_l2_force_0d_lockout, added for the opposite bug). So 0D -> force_sensitive=False -> every Force command rejects the player; the village-path failsafe doesn't fire for a fresh char. SAME ROOT also causes: +teach writing control/sense/alter into the SKILLS json column (force_sensitive stays False after CP spent — HIGH); 'train control/sense/alter' a silent dead-end (MEDIUM); test_character.yaml seeding Force skills into the skills column (MEDIUM).
design_calls_pending_brian.recommendation: Decouple force-sensitivity from the 0D dice value: persist it explicitly (a typed flag/column or an unambiguous non-dice sentinel) so a Force-sensitive-but-untrained char reads force_sensitive=True AND can attempt Force powers at base, WITHOUT the L2 spurious-sensitivity regression. Verify the correct WEG-D6 starting Force-skill representation (1D base vs 0D) against WEG40120 (the balance-touching part). Then fix the whole cluster (chargen blocker + teach-column + train-dead-end + test_character data) consistently.
design_calls_pending_brian.decision_needed: Approve the explicit-persisted-force-sensitivity approach + the WEG-D6 starting Force-skill value. NOTE: this slightly amends the 'force_sensitive is DERIVED from presence of control/sense/alter keys' invariant and may add a schema column.
design_calls_pending_brian.implementation_note: IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-22 (drop sidebar-contract-handoff-capture): WEG R&E confirms 1D starting (no schema change, no derivation change). api.py handle_submit + handle_create_character: DicePool(0,0) -> DicePool(1,0). padawan_master_training_commands._ensure_padawan_skills_one_die: writes to attrs not skills. cp_commands.TrainCommand: Force-skill intercept with teacher-redirect. test_character.yaml: control/sense/alter moved to top-level attributes. Status update (pending_brian -> resolved_recent) left to conductor per spec.

design_calls_pending_brian.title: Wire the Director AI to award CP ticks (award_ai_trickle) for quality RP, or leave it permanently dormant?
design_calls_pending_brian.origin: Guide_09 CP Progression authoritative pass (drop guide-09-cp-progression-authoritative, 2026-06-22): §2 documented an 'AI Evaluator Trickle' as one of four live income sources, but it is a PHANTOM FAUCET.
design_calls_pending_brian.premise_verified_at_head: engine/cp_engine.py CPEngine.award_ai_trickle (L313) is fully built (clamps to AI_MAX_TICKS_PER_EVAL=15, weekly-cap aware, graceful-drop) but a symbol-level grep of engine/parser/server/ai found ZERO callers — director.py never calls it. So no player can earn AI-eval ticks today. The guide was corrected to present the THREE active sources (passive/scene/kudos) and mark the AI trickle 'Planned — not yet active'.
design_calls_pending_brian.fork: Should the live Director (Claude Haiku, funded $30, $20/mo circuit-breaker) award a few CP ticks per evaluation for standout roleplay? If yes, the producer lives in engine/director.py (AVOID-LANE) and adds a metered-API eval cost per award; if no, leave award_ai_trickle dormant and keep the guide's 'Planned' framing.
design_calls_pending_brian.decision_needed: (1) Wire it pre-launch, defer post-launch, or never? (2) If wired — at what trigger (per Director turn? per flagged-quality pose?) and what cost ceiling, given the $20/mo Director cap already covers faction-turn news.

design_calls_pending_brian.title: Environmental-hazard debuffs (dehydration / toxic exposure) are permanent with no in-game cure — intended, or wire a recovery path?
design_calls_pending_brian.origin: Guide_24 Encounters & Hazards authoritative pass (drop guide-24-encounters-hazards-authoritative, 2026-06-22): the guide claimed dehydration clears when you 'drink water or leave the heat' and toxic exposure when you 'equip a breath mask or leave' — both are phantom recovery paths.
design_calls_pending_brian.premise_verified_at_head: engine/buffs.py BUFF_TEMPLATES['dehydration'] and ['toxic_exposure'] are duration_seconds:0 (is_expired() returns False = permanent), and engine/buffs.py remove_buff() has ZERO callers across engine/parser/server (symbol grep). engine/hazards.py mitigation only PREVENTS the check (returns mitigated:True without touching existing buffs) — carrying a canteen/mask never clears stacks already taken. No 'drink'/'rest'/medical/relog path resets active_buffs. So once dehydration (up to -1D STR / -1D DEX at 3 stacks) or toxic exposure (-1D STR) lands, it is permanent. The guide was corrected to a prevention-not-cure framing.
design_calls_pending_brian.secondary_finding: Severity scaling looks double-applied: set_room_hazard stores difficulty = base + (severity-1)*3, then check_hazard_for_character adds (severity-1)*3 again — net +6 per severity tier, not the apparent +3. Harmless at the default severity 1 (the common case), but worth confirming intent.
design_calls_pending_brian.fork: hazards.py is NOT avoid-lane, but adding a cure mechanic is a feature/design decision. (A) wire remove_buff to a recovery action (e.g. a 'drink' verb that consumes a water_canteen and clears 1 dehydration stack; rest/medical for toxic); (B) give the debuffs a time-decay duration; (C) intended-permanent — keep as a hard prevention-first punishment and accept the guide's prevention framing as final.
design_calls_pending_brian.decision_needed: Pick A/B/C for the cure path, and confirm whether the per-severity difficulty double-scaling is intended.

design_calls_pending_brian.title: Space anomalies: 6 of 7 types have no engagement command, and the engine's own scan readout tells players to type 'course anomaly <id>', which is unwired
design_calls_pending_brian.origin: Guide_24 Encounters & Hazards authoritative pass (2026-06-22): the guide promised an `investigate <anomaly_id>` flow + rich per-type resolution; verifying against HEAD showed almost none of it is wired.
design_calls_pending_brian.premise_verified_at_head: engine/space_anomalies.py defines 7 anomaly types (derelict, distress, cache, pirates, mineral_vein, imperial->'Republic Dead Drop', mynock) and every full scan readout instructs 'Type course anomaly {id}' to engage. But parser/space_commands.py CourseCommand only handles `course <zone>` / `course cancel` (_match_destination matches adjacent ZONES only) -> `course anomaly 3` fails as 'not an adjacent zone'. The ONLY wired anomaly engagement is SalvageCommand (anomaly_type=='derelict' + combat wrecks). distress / cache / pirates / mineral_vein / dead-drop / mynock have NO resolution command; their slicing-decode / cache-bypass / nest-combat / detach flows are unbuilt. The guide was rewritten to the live loop (scan -> deepscan <id> -> salvage derelicts) and the phantom `investigate <anomaly_id>` removed.
design_calls_pending_brian.lane_note: space_commands.py / the space layer is the interactive conductor's QA lane — flagging here, NOT fixing, to avoid collision.
design_calls_pending_brian.fork: (A) Build the missing per-type engagement (a real `course anomaly <id>` or a dedicated verb routed per anomaly_type), making the readouts true; (B) trim the anomaly system to detection + derelict-salvage at launch and fix the misleading 'course anomaly {id}' readout text to match what's wired; (C) leave as-is post-launch.
design_calls_pending_brian.decision_needed: Pick A/B/C; if anything pre-launch, it belongs to the space/conductor lane, not the unattended loops.

design_calls_pending_brian.title: Ship's-Log milestone titles (Explorer 16 zones / Galactic Traveler 4 planets / Spotter 19 ships) and their 'All N ...' flavor text are stale against the June space rework's larger galaxy graph
design_calls_pending_brian.origin: Guide_05 Space Systems authoritative pass (2026-06-23): documenting the live galaxy showed the title thresholds no longer match the zone/planet counts the rework produced.
design_calls_pending_brian.premise_verified_at_head: data/worlds/clone_wars/space_zones.yaml now defines 6 planets (tatooine, nar_shaddaa, coruscant, kuat, kamino, geonosis) x dock/orbit/deep_space = 18 planet zones, plus 6 hyperspace lanes + 4 wildspace zones = 28 zones total. But engine/ships_log.py MILESTONES still threshold Explorer at 16 zones with msg 'All 16 zones charted', Galactic Traveler at 4 planets with msg 'Touched down on all 4 planets', and Spotter at 19 ship types (the live CW registry now has 21 distinct hulls / 23 keys). The titles still FIRE (they are milestone counters, not gated on a live total) but the thresholds are no longer 'all', and the flavor text is factually wrong. The guide documents the thresholds honestly as milestones and does not claim they equal the galaxy total.
design_calls_pending_brian.lane_note: engine/ships_log.py is not avoid-lane, but the threshold VALUES are a balance/design call and the space layer is the interactive conductor's QA lane -- flagging here, not guessing.
design_calls_pending_brian.fork: (A) Re-tune to the new graph as true completion (Explorer 28 zones, Galactic Traveler 6 planets, Spotter 21 ships) + fix the 'All N' flavor; (B) keep the current thresholds as mid-game milestones and reword the flavor so it no longer says 'all'; (C) leave as-is post-launch.
design_calls_pending_brian.decision_needed: Pick A/B/C; if pre-launch, it belongs to the space/conductor lane, not the unattended loops.

design_calls_resolved_recent.raised_by: drop qa-h1-item-registry (H1 fix; investigation Option B)
design_calls_resolved_recent.severity: design (post-launch consolidation; NOT launch-blocking -- H1 resolved by extending weapons.yaml)
design_calls_resolved_recent.status: DEFERRED post-launch (Brian: best/most-complete chose EXTEND for launch). The launch fix authored the 14 combat-class items into the existing weapons.yaml WeaponRegistry (which already holds armor as type:armor). The cleaner long-term model is a unified data/items.yaml + ItemRegistry with an item_type discriminator (weapon/armor/consumable/quest/misc) that the equip/wear/use/grant consumers all read -- giving the ~24 misc/quest props a typed home + room for future use-handlers. Investigation flagged it as the invariant-gated new-top-level-system (extend-don't-add) with a large pre-launch regression surface (re-homes 65 existing rows, rewires ~30 get_weapon_registry call sites) -> defer post-launch.
design_calls_resolved_recent.fork: Post-launch: consolidate weapons.yaml + consumables.yaml + the narrative-prop dicts into one unified ItemRegistry with an item_type discriminator?
design_calls_resolved_recent.related: QA_FINDINGS_2026-06-16.md H1; engine/weapons.py WeaponRegistry; engine/consumables.py; engine/items.py ItemInstance

design_calls_resolved_recent.severity: design (the 'redeemed' achievement is unwinnable; not blocking launch core)
design_calls_resolved_recent.status: RESOLVED 2026-06-16 (Brian): OPTION B -- DEFER post-launch. No DSP-atonement mechanic at launch; dark_side_points stays a one-way ratchet, so the redeemed achievement + on_dark_side_atoned hook + the dsp_hunter atonement-escape branch stay dormant (documented, NOT wired -- wiring to an arbitrary seam would be a phantom producer). Revisit post-launch if Jedi redemption becomes a theme; then it is a small drop (one atonement action + the hook wire). A grooming pass can relocate this to design_calls_resolved_recent.
design_calls_resolved_recent.premise_verified_at_head: Confirmed at HEAD: dark_side_points is only ever INCREMENTED (engine/force_powers.py += 1 at L465/L496); a grep of engine/ + parser/ found NO code path that REDUCES dark_side_points. engine.achievements.on_dark_side_atoned (L601) + the 'redeemed' achievement (trigger event dark_side_atoned) therefore have no producer. engine/dsp_hunter.py references 'atonement' only as 'drop back under the wanted threshold' -- but since nothing lowers DSP, that escape path is itself unreachable.
design_calls_resolved_recent.fork: Should a DSP-atonement (Jedi redemption) mechanic ship pre-launch so dark_side_points can DECREASE? Without it the 'redeemed' achievement + on_dark_side_atoned hook are permanently stranded and the dsp_hunter 'atonement ends the pursuit' branch can never fire.
options: A) Build a minimal atonement action (e.g. a Jedi meditation/penance that removes 1 DSP under conditions) + wire on_dark_side_atoned at that seam. Closes the achievement AND activates the dsp_hunter atonement escape. Small drop.
B) Defer post-launch: leave 'redeemed' unwinnable for now (documented); DSP stays a one-way ratchet at launch.
C) Drop the 'redeemed' achievement + on_dark_side_atoned hook entirely if redemption is not a launch theme.
design_calls_resolved_recent.recommendation: Lean B for launch unless Jedi redemption is a desired launch theme -- self-contained feature, not a blocker, achievement gap is cosmetic. If A is wanted it is a small follow-up (one atonement action + the hook wire). Do NOT wire on_dark_side_atoned to an arbitrary seam (phantom producer).

design_calls_resolved_recent.raised_by: drop pm-leave-master / T3.13 gap-check (HANDOFF_t313_padawan_master_gapcheck_2026-06-14.md, areas P5/P11)
design_calls_resolved_recent.severity: design (gates the Master-approval-weight feature P5; not blocking launch core)
design_calls_resolved_recent.status: RESOLVED + SHIPPED 2026-06-14 (drop pm-authorize): OPTION C (pre-authorization-only at launch) — Brian ('go with your recommendations'). BUILT: +authorize <padawan> <category> [off] in parser/padawan_master_trials.py (AuthorizeCommand) stores a standing master_authorizations JSON list on the Padawan's chargen_notes (mirrors +endorse; no new table/schema). Categories offworld/powers/trials map to the §5.3 gated actions. Per OPT C the per-action +approve/+deny block-and-wait flow and the 7-day absence auto-approve are SKIPPED at launch (grow to full §5.3 / option D post-launch). Real consumers at launch: the +trials display Endorsement line (standing/ready/none) + the +authorize list views; the mechanical action-gates (offworld travel, field Force-power use) read these standing grants when/where those commands are built. tests/test_pm_authorize.py (16) green; full PM suite 165 green. A grooming pass can move this to design_calls_resolved_recent.
design_calls_resolved_recent.premise_verified_at_head: Confirmed at HEAD: the Padawan/Master design §5.3 specs Master-approval-gated Padawan actions via +approve/+deny/+authorize, but NONE of those three commands exist at HEAD (grepped the 3 padawan_master parser files + engine), and there is NO pending-approval store or action-gating layer. The bond/trials core (+bond/+master/+padawan/+teach/+spar/+trials/+endorse/+knight/+release/+leave-master) IS built.
design_calls_resolved_recent.fork: Before building the approval mechanics (P5), decide: (a) WHERE are pending approvals tracked, and (b) HOW MUCH gating ships at launch? §5.3 gates 3 things (leaving Coruscant for a non-sanctioned mission, using an unauthorized Force power in the field, initiating a Trial) and adds a 7-day master-absence auto-approve.
options: A) IN-MEMORY pending-approval store (a dict keyed by padawan+action, TTL'd) — simplest, no schema; approvals lost on restart (acceptable for ephemeral action-gates).
B) DB-backed pending_approvals table — survives restart; heavier; needed only if approvals must persist across sessions.

C) MINIMAL launch: ship +authorize (Master pre-authorizes a category, stored on the bond/char) only, and SKIP the per-action +approve/+deny block-and-wait flow at launch (the gated actions just check the pre-authorization). Avoids the cross-cutting action-interception layer entirely.
D) FULL §5.3: per-action block-pending-approval + +approve/+deny + +authorize pre-auth + 7-day absence auto-approve.
design_calls_resolved_recent.recommendation: Lean C for launch (pre-authorization categories only — low complexity, no action-interception layer, no new table) then grow to D post-launch if the per-action drama is wanted. If full §5.3 is desired at launch, option A's in-memory store is enough. Do NOT blind-build the action-interception layer without this call.

design_calls_resolved_recent.raised_by: drop cities-client-polish / T3.14 gap-check (HANDOFF_t314_cities_gapcheck_2026-06-14.md, area C8)
design_calls_resolved_recent.status: RESOLVED + SHIPPED 2026-06-14: OPTION A (align to spec) — Brian ('go with your recommendations'). Built in drop cities-dissolution-refund: engine/player_cities.py refund = 25% of expansion-room claim costs (expansion_rooms * EXPANSION_CLAIM_COST * 25 // 100); DISSOLUTION_REFUND_PCT 50->25; no-expansion city now refunds 0 (HQ cost sunk). tests/test_cities_phase1.py updated (refund tracks expansion rooms, not HQ tier). A grooming pass can archive this entry.
design_calls_resolved_recent.premise_verified_at_head: Confirmed at HEAD: engine/player_cities.py defines DISSOLUTION_REFUND_PCT=50 and dissolve_city() refunds 50% of FOUNDING_COSTS[hq_type] (the HQ founding cost). The design doc player_cities_design_v1_2.md §8.3 specifies the refund as '25% of expansion-room claim costs' (i.e. 25% of the sum of EXPANSION_CLAIM_COST=5000 per claimed room, NOT a fraction of the HQ founding cost). Both the percentage (50 vs 25) and the base (founding cost vs expansion-room costs) diverge.
design_calls_resolved_recent.fork: Align the dissolution refund to spec §8.3 (25% of expansion-room claim costs), or keep the current behavior (50% of HQ founding cost)? This is a credit-faucet shape on city teardown.
options: A) Align to spec: refund = 25% * (claimed_expansion_rooms * EXPANSION_CLAIM_COST). Smaller, scales with how much was built; matches the design intent that founding cost is mostly sunk.
B) Keep current: 50% of HQ founding cost (outpost 25k / chapter_house 75k / fortress 200k -> 12.5k/37.5k/100k flat refund). Larger, independent of expansion.
C) A hybrid / different number (Brian specifies).
design_calls_resolved_recent.recommendation: Lean A (align to the documented design) unless Brian intends cities to be cheap to churn. Either way it's a one-function change in dissolve_city() + the constant; trivial to implement once decided. Left unbuilt pending the call (do not guess at refund economics).

design_calls_resolved_recent.status: EXECUTED 2026-06-14 (drop tutorial-v2-gcw-chains-delete) — OPTION A (DELETE), Brian. REBEL_CELL/IMPERIAL_SERVICE definitions+TOTALs, their check_profession_chains dispatch blocks, the get_chain_status_lines rows, _can_start_rebel_cell/_can_start_imperial_service, and the _GCW_PROFESSION_CHAINS_ENABLED flag are removed from engine/tutorial_v2.py (~318 lines); tests/test_tutorial_v2_era_cleanness.py now asserts the symbols are GONE (was: dormant). The rest of the profession-chain system stays live. This entry can be archived out of the pending array by a grooming pass.
design_calls_resolved_recent.premise_verified_at_head: Confirmed at HEAD: engine/tutorial_v2.py defines REBEL_CELL + IMPERIAL_SERVICE — full 5-step Galactic-Civil-War profession chains (Rebel Alliance recruitment via 'Fulcrum'; Imperial Service under 'Sergeant Kreel'). check_profession_chains() (the dispatch) is LIVE — wired from parser/mission_commands.py, parser/npc_commands.py (talk_rebel_contact), parser/smuggling_commands.py, parser/space_commands.py. Pre-fix, _can_start_rebel_cell/_can_start_imperial_service gated ONLY on missions_complete>=2 (NO era check), and get_chain_status_lines unconditionally listed both chains — so a Clone Wars player completing 2 missions was offered off-era GCW content. An AST scan of the file found 53 banned-era-token strings; the pre-existing era-cleanness test (tests/test_laneb_era_cleanness.py) is deliberately surgical (curated (file,string) pairs) and never scanned this file (same partial-coverage blind spot the wilderness/chain drops hit).
design_calls_resolved_recent.interim_mitigation_shipped: This drop made the violation UNREACHABLE without destroying content: added module flag _GCW_PROFESSION_CHAINS_ENABLED=False gating both _can_start_* to return False and hiding both rows from get_chain_status_lines; reworded the 5 scattered LIVE off-era barks (DISCOVERY_QUESTS Nar Shaddaa/Kessel/Corellia welcomes, Smuggler's Run step 2 task, Kessa's Kreel teaser) to CW-clean flavor (Imperial->Republic; Empire->Hutt cartels; Rebel sympathizers->independents; Imperial patrol->Republic customs patrol). Guard: tests/test_tutorial_v2_era_cleanness.py walks the LIVE chain objects with no allow-list + pins the two chains dormant. REBEL_CELL/IMPERIAL_SERVICE definitions + dispatch remain in source (unreachable).
design_calls_resolved_recent.fork: What is the PERMANENT disposition of the two dormant GCW chains (REBEL_CELL, IMPERIAL_SERVICE)? They are currently inert but their off-era source strings still live in engine/tutorial_v2.py.
options: A) DELETE them outright (definitions + dispatch + status + _can_start + the GCW flag) — cleanest era-cleanness, loses ~190 lines of GCW content that would need full re-authoring if a GCW era is ever added.
B) KEEP dormant as-is (current state) — content preserved for a future GCW era behind the flag; off-era strings remain in source but unreachable. Lowest effort, mild source-hygiene cost.

C) CW-REWRITE them into era-appropriate Clone Wars profession chains (e.g. a Republic Intelligence arc replacing Imperial Service; a Separatist-sympathizer or neutral-faction arc replacing Rebel Cell) — most content value, most effort, needs design + a reachability/era pass.

design_calls_resolved_recent.raised_by: T3.21 security pre-pass (HANDOFF_t3_21_security_optimization_2026-06-13.md Blocker 1 / IDOR), re-audited against HEAD by the Opus solo overnight session
design_calls_resolved_recent.severity: design + privacy (not blocking launch core; should be ruled before a public soft-launch)
design_calls_resolved_recent.fork: The audit flagged web_portal.handle_character returning online status + achievement_count + scene_count for ANY char_id UNAUTHENTICATED as an IDOR 'live who is online map' (stalking vector). RE-AUDIT CONTEXT the timeslice missed: the character DIRECTORY endpoint (handle_characters) is ALSO fully public and already returns 'online' for every listed character BY DESIGN. So this is a PRODUCT decision, not a pure bug: should a player's online status + activity counts be PUBLIC (a social 'who is around' feature, as the directory implements today) or PRIVATE (anti-stalking)? Gating only handle_character would be inconsistent and would not close the directory exposure.
design_calls_resolved_recent.options: A) PUBLIC by design -- keep directory + profile as-is, close the IDOR as not-a-bug, optionally add enumeration rate-limiting to blunt mass-scraping. B) AUTH-GATED (RECOMMENDED) -- require any logged-in account to see online/activity on BOTH the directory and the profile; kills unauthenticated mass-enumeration while keeping a social who-is-online for real players; needs handle_characters to also require auth. C) OWNER/ADMIN-ONLY -- the audit's literal recommendation; most private but removes the social directory feature.
design_calls_resolved_recent.status: RESOLVED 2026-06-16 (Brian): OPTION A -- PUBLIC BY DESIGN. Online/activity status (the online flag + achievement_count + scene_count on web_portal.handle_character, and the 'online' field on the public handle_characters directory) is intentionally public as a social 'who is around' feature; the directory already exposes it by design. The audit's IDOR/stalking flag is therefore a NOT-A-BUG product decision, not a vulnerability -- leave handle_character + the directory online/activity fields AS-IS; NO auth-gating work. (Optional, non-blocking future hardening: enumeration rate-limiting to blunt mass-scraping.) All three T3.21 blockers are now closed: Blocker 2 (per-IP login throttle) + the faction-membership column-name bug (drop t321-login-throttle), Blocker 3 (admin re-validation + admin_audit, schema v45; drop t321-admin-audit), and this Blocker 1 IDOR (resolved not-a-bug). Entry retained here as a RESOLVED record; a future grooming pass may relocate it to design_calls_resolved_recent.

RESOLVED 2026-06-17 (Brian: best/most-complete, go-with-recommendations): OPTION A -- the live leader/Director post_faction_mission DB path is canonical. Built (drop qa-h2-faction-missions): post_faction_mission writes a full slug-keyed Mission.to_dict() blob with a real destination_room_id (leader->faction HQ room; Director->target-zone room) so the existing accept->complete->adjust_credits->rep funnel works; rep gate enforced at accept; faction missions excluded from the public board; slug/footer fixed; inject helper relocated to engine.missions (no engine->parser inversion). design_calls_resolved_recent.raised_by: drop qa-m3-shop-bare-list (Opus loop); QA finding H2 in docs/design/QA_FINDINGS_2026-06-16.md, investigated against HEAD before picking the fire's drop
design_calls_resolved_recent.severity: HIGH (faction missions are un-acceptable AND un-completable end-to-end; a member who posts one dead-ends) -- but NOT a clean loop-safe mechanical fix; reconciling two systems + completion semantics is a design call, and the canonical fix touches an avoid-lane file
design_calls_resolved_recent.premise_verified_at_head: Confirmed at HEAD that TWO independent faction-mission systems coexist and neither yields an acceptable+completable mission: (1) engine/missions.py generate_faction_mission() + available_missions_for_char() produce in-memory Missions with SLUG ids, faction_code, and a rep gate -- these would work through the existing `accept <slug>` board path, BUT they are referenced ONLY by tests/docs (grep: no live producer wires them into the board; the public generate_board() never calls generate_faction_mission). UNWIRED. (2) The LIVE path: db.post_faction_mission() (db/database.py:5384, called by parser/faction_leader_commands._handle_mission `faction mission create` AND engine/director.py) INSERTs a missions row with an INTEGER autoincrement id and NO `data` JSON blob -> the row's data takes the column default '{}'. The in-memory board loader (MissionBoard._load_from_db) does Mission.from_dict(json.loads(row['data'])) -> from_dict({}) KeyErrors -> the row is DISCARDED, so it never appears on the public `missions`/`accept` board (defect #1). It IS shown by `faction missions` (parser/faction_commands._cmd_missions via db.get_faction_missions) but with the INTEGER id, and the footer text says 'mission accept <id>' while the real verb is bare `accept` (defect #2; `mission accept N` parses as the ActiveMissionCommand which ignores args). And `accept N` looks up the SLUG-keyed in-memory board -> 'No mission N' (defect #3). On top of all three: a leader-posted faction mission carries NO destination/destination_room_id and only a free-text description, so even if made acceptable it can NEVER pass CompleteMissionCommand._check_ground_destination -> still un-completable.
design_calls_resolved_recent.fork: Which faction-mission system is canonical, and how should a leader-posted (and Director-posted) faction mission be ACCEPTED and COMPLETED? The clean fix (make post_faction_mission write a real slug-keyed `data` blob so the board picks it up + show the slug in `faction missions` + fix the footer) edits db/database.py, which is AVOID-LANE while the B1 row_factory work is in-flight; and it does not address that these missions have no destination/completion criteria.
options: A) Make db.post_faction_mission write a full Mission.to_dict() `data` blob (slug id, faction_code from faction_id, a real destination chosen from rooms, objective from the description) so faction missions flow through the EXISTING board accept/complete path; fix `faction missions` to show the slug id and the footer to say `accept <id>`. Requires the db edit (avoid-lane today) + a destination/completion model for leader-posted missions. Reconciles toward system (2); retires the unwired generate_faction_mission OR routes it through the same writer.

B) Adopt the UNWIRED generate_faction_mission()/available_missions_for_char() as canonical: wire them into the board refresh (rep-gated faction missions appear on the public board, accepted via `accept <slug>`, completed via the normal destination path since they DO carry a destination), and make `faction mission create` (+ the Director) call THAT generator instead of post_faction_mission. Deletes the dead-end DB path. Larger, but every piece already exists and is tested; no avoid-lane db edit needed beyond removing the post_faction_mission call sites (one is in director.py = avoid-lane).

C) Give faction missions their OWN self-contained accept/complete verbs that read the integer-id DB rows directly (parser-only), with a defined completion criterion (e.g. complete-on-turn-in at the faction HQ, or a Director-resolved outcome). Keeps the two systems separate; most new surface.
design_calls_resolved_recent.recommendation: Lean B (the unwired rep-gated generator is the better-designed system, fully tested, carries destinations, and needs no avoid-lane db write except removing post_faction_mission call sites). If B is too broad for one drop, A is the smaller reconciliation but is BLOCKED until db/database.py leaves the B1 avoid-lane and still needs a completion model. Do NOT ship a partial 'make accept work' fix that leaves completion broken (it just relocates the dead-end). NOT guessed; left for Brian.
design_calls_resolved_recent.related: QA_FINDINGS_2026-06-16.md H2; engine/missions.py (generate_faction_mission/available_missions_for_char/MissionBoard._load_from_db); db/database.py post_faction_mission/get_faction_missions; parser/faction_leader_commands._handle_mission; parser/faction_commands._cmd_missions; parser/mission_commands AcceptMissionCommand/CompleteMissionCommand; engine/director.py (a post_faction_mission caller = avoid-lane)

design_calls_resolved_recent.resolution: SHIP, BIAS LOW (Brian, 2026-06-13). TD.ENCOUNTER_COUNT_RANGE_IGNORED: wilderness encounters authored count as a [lo,hi] range but creature_library.creature_spawn_count could not parse a list (int([4,6]) raised -> swallowed -> always fell back to the creature pack_count minimum), so EVERY ranged encounter (the existing 18 too) spawned its floor. Honoring the range raises spawn counts galaxy-wide -> a conscious balance call. Brian ruled: fix the bug but tune conservatively. Implemented engine/creature_library._roll_low_biased(lo,hi) = min(randint,randint) (mean ~a third up the range, e.g. [4,6]->~4.56 vs uniform 5.0 vs old always-4); creature_spawn_count now routes a 2-list count through it (scalar/edge/malformed paths unchanged; reversed range sorted; 1-list treated as scalar). Still often spawns the minimum, so the difficulty creep is gentle. Verified: new tests/test_encounter_count_range.py (14 — bounds, low-bias mean below uniform midpoint, full-range span, no-longer-always-min, reversed/scalar/malformed/no-count edges, end-to-end through spawn_encounter_creatures) + spawner/resolution regression (34 together). Parallel-safe: engine/creature_library.py (not in either session's avoid-set) + new test. drop encounter-count-range.
design_calls_resolved_recent.mapping_decided_by_me: Bias mechanism (min-of-two uniform rolls) chosen under decide+build+log to satisfy 'bias low' deterministically and without a magic skew constant: it provably keeps the mean below the uniform midpoint while still spanning the full authored range and frequently returning the floor (so no encounter ever spawns FEWER than before, and the cap is the authored hi). pack_count minimums were already tuned as sane floors under the old always-min behavior, so they remain valid as the low end.

design_calls_resolved_recent.priority: LOW (blocks only a fully-green full suite, via two non-production tooling files owned by the parallel guides-rework session)
design_calls_resolved_recent.question: Cross-session collision surfaced by the xdist full-suite triage. tests/test_session38.py::TestSilentExceptInvariant::test_no_silent_except_pass_in_production walks the whole tree (skips only venv/__pycache__/tests/.git) and flags two UNTRACKED files from the guides-rework session — tools/guide_lint.py:39 and tools/split_guide_dev_track.py:35. HEAD-verified: both use the benign console-bootstrap idiom `for s in (stdout,stderr): try: getattr(sys,s).reconfigure(encoding='utf-8') except Exception: pass`; the reconfigure idiom appears in NO other .py in the tree (only these 2); the scanner has no allowlist/noqa mechanism. The full pytest suite cannot go green while these sit in the working tree. The triage session does not own these files (per the xdist handoff: don't touch other sessions' uncommitted strays) — Brian decided 2026-06-13 to FLAG, not touch. NOTE: the one REAL production silent-except this invariant caught (parser/commissary_commands.py — bare except:pass at :197 + two comment-masked siblings at :117/:146) was already fixed by the triage session: all three now log.debug(..., exc_info=True) per the bounty/inventory/crafting best-effort idiom.
design_calls_resolved_recent.recommendation: Owner = the guides-rework session. Resolve by (A) giving both reconfigure blocks a real `log.debug('stream reconfigure failed', exc_info=True)` (matches the codebase best-effort idiom; keeps the invariant strict with zero carve-outs; 2-line change in each owning file) — RECOMMENDED — or (B) teach the test_session38 scanner to skip a try/except:pass whose body is only a `sys.std*.reconfigure(...)` console-UTF-8 bootstrap (a known-safe class). Rec (A): a hygiene invariant with no exceptions is more durable than one with a special case, and the cost is trivial. Until then the full suite carries exactly these 2 reds, both isolated to non-production tooling.
design_calls_resolved_recent.logged: 2026-06-13 (xdist full-suite triage session; F4 of 4 — the only residual outside the triage session's lane)
design_calls_resolved_recent.resolution: FIXED + CLOSED 2026-06-13 (Brian Ruling 6). The two guide-tool reconfigure blocks got a justified non-pass body; test_no_silent_except_pass_in_production is green. Verified at HEAD this session.

design_calls_resolved_recent.question: Restraint/handcuff system (CRAFT.HOOK long-pole). Today: combat grapple has an EPHEMERAL CombatBracket.restraint (Str-vs-Str escape, combat-only, not persisted); no handcuff items exist. Needs a restraint STATE model, escape checks, and PvP-consent norms before any content ships.
design_calls_resolved_recent.recommendation: (1) Persistent character-level restraint state {applied_by, item_key, escape_difficulty} — survives logout (a cuffed prisoner stays cuffed), distinct from combat's ephemeral grapple. (2) Blocks attack/move/equip; allows social verbs (mirror the grapple restriction set). (3) Escape = WEG Strength (or Security to pick the lock) check vs. difficulty, as an action; 'unlock' releases. (4) PvP-consent (the crux): REUSE the existing PvP/security-zone gate — you may only cuff a player you could legally attack, and only one already DEFEATED/incapacitated (no full-health griefing); NPCs unrestricted. No new consent system. Decision for Brian: persistent state (rec yes) + reuse-existing-PvP-gate (rec yes). Sequence AFTER the equipment-instance migration (touches Character state + equip).
design_calls_resolved_recent.status: UNBLOCKED — PvP norm decided (consent/defeat-gated); build per the recommendation
design_calls_resolved_recent.pvp_consent_resolution: RESOLVED 2026-06-13 (Brian): restraints are CONSENT/DEFEAT-GATED — a PC may only cuff another PC who CONSENTS or who has been DEFEATED (incapacitated/surrendered in combat), never a healthy unwilling PC. Capture is an outcome of winning a fight or a willing RP scene, not a surprise grief vector. The rest of the recommendation (persistent character-level restraint state {applied_by, item_key, escape_difficulty} surviving logout; blocks attack/move/equip, allows social verbs; escape = WEG Strength check) stands — build per that. Now UNBLOCKED for implementation under the decide+build charter.
design_calls_resolved_recent.resolution: BUILT 2026-06-13 (drops 48+49). Consent/defeat-gated per Brian; persistent attributes.restraint state (no migration); escape=lifting(Strength)-vs-15; binders sink; captor-or-admin release; 6 gates; v1 PCs-only. CRAFT.HOOK.restraints closed.

Brian set a standing autonomy charter for running toward launch: (1) DECIDE+BUILD+LOG — make design/balance/economy calls myself, log them, stop only for the truly irreversible (live-DB-breaking schema, content deletion, external sends). (2) BALANCE = CONSERVATIVE default (err tight/low when unsure, pair with T3.19 tunables for live retune). (3) SEQUENCE = features-first, harden-last (cluster T3.19/20/21 + arch reconciliation at the end). (4) Restraints PvP = consent/defeat-gated. Recorded in memory overnight-autonomy-posture. This supersedes the 'log every fork + wait' base workflow for non-irreversible work.

design_calls_resolved_recent.premise: Option A (crafted quality SHOULD affect consumable combat output, like weapons/armor) is already Brian-greenlit under CRAFT.quality_combat_read_armor_consumables. What's OPEN is the POTENCY MODEL + scope — a balance call, not mechanical. HEAD-verified: stims grant +1D/+2D = 3-6 pips of stat_modifier via add_buff (engine/buffs.py), far above the weapon ±2-pip band; medpacs heal discrete wound levels (1/2/1) from _STIM_CATALOG in medical_commands.py; consumables store as a bare int count today (no quality persisted past the craft message).
design_calls_resolved_recent.question: (1) Potency model: P1 = pip-delta-on-base (mirror weapons/armor — q-band [-1..+2] added to the buff's pip magnitude, hard-capped +2; a q95 stim = +1D+2 instead of +1D) vs P2 = quality-factor multiplier (mirror starship _quality_factor — scale the whole modifier ×0.5/0.75/1.0; reduces low-q rather than boosting high). (2) Do MEDPAC heal levels scale with quality at all? Recommend NO (keep discrete 1/2/1; route quality to a First-Aid roll bonus instead) — scaling heal magnitude blows up balance + churns the gundark drop-A heal pins. (3) Per-key vs per-unit quality when a stack mixes q40+q90 stims? Recommend per-key, max-on-recraft. (4) breaching_charge quality — recommend inert (ordnance, no potency mechanic).
design_calls_resolved_recent.recommendation: P1 pip-delta cap +2 (consistent with the ratified weapon/armor band + the 'hard-capped, no power creep' greenlight); medpac heal stays discrete with quality -> First-Aid roll bonus; per-key max-on-recraft; breaching_charge inert. I'll write a short design doc (docs/design/consumable_quality_potency_v1.md) laying this out with the balance math before building, per Brian's 'design doc first if you need it' steer.
design_calls_resolved_recent.resolution: RESOLVED 2026-06-13 on the recommended defaults (Brian: 'move forward with the consumable work… don't stall'). Built in drop 45: P1 pip-delta model, cap +1 (tighter than armor/weapon +2 for the small 3-pip stim base); medpac heal stays discrete (quality -> First-Aid roll bonus, not magnitude); per-key quality, max-on-recraft; breaching_charge quality inert. Power-creep guard pinned (q95 stim < adrenaline base). Brian can still retune any of the 4 knobs — all are single-constant changes in crafted_consumable_potency_pips or the medical_commands hook.

design_calls_resolved_recent.question: Crafted QUALITY reaches combat for WEAPONS ONLY (Drop 19: engine/items.py crafted_combat_pips reads the equipped ItemInstance quality -> damage/accuracy, hard-capped). For ARMOR and CONSUMABLES, quality is stored-but-DECORATION: get_armor_protection (engine/character.py:457) reads only the bare worn_armor KEY (a q95 vest soaks like a q40), and crafted consumables carry no potency-from-quality. Surfaced by the crafting-integration review (2026-06-13, item 3). Option A = make them real (consistent with the Drop-19 weapon precedent); Option B = stop rendering the promise (like the stat_bonus message just dropped in drop 43). Which?
design_calls_resolved_recent.recommendation: This is a genuine BALANCE fork, not mechanical — armor-soak-by-quality + consumable-potency-by-quality are power-creep-sensitive (a q95 armor soaking far above vendor baseline shifts combat math). NOT safe to guess. If A (make real): mirror the weapon pip path (read the INSTANCE, hard-cap the quality delta like the +1D weapon cap) and BUNDLE the armor half with the TD.EQUIPMENT_CHARACTER_HOLDS_KEYS_NOT_INSTANCES migration (get_armor_protection needs the instance, not the key) — land it solo on a quiet combat window, never concurrent with combat/equipment edits (review flagged combat.py/items.py/character.py as HIGH collision). The consumable half touches parser/crafting_commands.py:1296 (the consumable-deliver branch) + medical_commands.py and changes the consumable storage shape (count -> quality-bearing) — sequence AFTER any in-flight crafting edits. If B: drop the quality-implies-better framing for armor/consumables. Rec: A for armor (precedent + player expectation), but as its own carefully-sequenced drop; decide consumables separately (potion-potency power creep is the riskier half).
design_calls_resolved_recent.logged: 2026-06-13 (crafting-integration review item 3; review doc HANDOFF_crafting_integration_review_2026-06-13.md). Per the main-session watch protocol: STOP+log, do not blind-integrate (HIGH combat-file collision + balance call).
design_calls_resolved_recent.status: DECIDED 2026-06-13 — Brian: OPTION A, quality should absolutely matter for armor AND consumables (precedent-consistent with weapons). NOT YET BUILT — this is a careful, combat-collision-prone drop, greenlit with direction: (armor) read the worn-armor INSTANCE quality in get_armor_protection and fold a HARD-CAPPED quality delta into soak (mirror the weapon +1D pip-cap discipline so no power creep); BUNDLE with the TD.EQUIPMENT_CHARACTER_HOLDS_KEYS_NOT_INSTANCES migration (get_armor_protection needs the instance, not the bare key) and land solo on a quiet combat window (review flagged combat.py/items.py/character.py as HIGH collision). (consumables) thread quality into potency (heal amount / buff magnitude), capped; touches the consumable storage shape (count -> quality-bearing) + medical_commands.py, sequence AFTER any in-flight crafting edits. Build as its own drop, not blind same-day. Promoted to a tier_1/tier_2 build item.
design_calls_resolved_recent.resolution: RESOLVED Option A (Brian: 'quality should absolutely matter'). ARMOR half shipped drop 44. CONSUMABLE half's remaining OPEN question is the potency MODEL — re-logged as CRAFT.consumable_quality_potency_model. This umbrella fork is closed.

ROOT-FIXED (Drop 22). Diagnosed via a post-pytest threading.enumerate() audit: the suite completes (8333 passed) but hangs at process EXIT because aiosqlite spawns one NON-DAEMON threading.Thread(target=_connection_worker_thread) per connection (aiosqlite/core.py:90, v0.22.1), and tests that open a Database without closing it leave that thread alive (blocked on its op-queue), blocking interpreter exit (test_cities_phase4b alone leaked 15). FIX: tests/conftest.py wraps aiosqlite.Connection.__init__ to set self._thread.daemon = True at import, so a leaked connection can't block exit and the interpreter exits naturally with full teardown. Proven: cities + harness smoke (all previously hung) return rc=0 on their own (26 passed, no env var, no timeout-kill). The interim SW_MUSH_HARD_EXIT os._exit band-aid (conftest + run_all_tests.bat) was REMOVED. Residual hygiene: tests still don't close their connections (now harmless since the threads are daemon); a later pass could close them in teardown.

Option b (data-driven) delivered — Drop 23. CommandParser.process now merges a data-driven set into its routable-direction check: engine.wilderness_movement.get_custom_edge_directions() harvests every wilderness-edge direction_from_room word from the region YAMLs (currently {deeper, east}; 'deeper' is the Coruscant Underworld edge that previously hit 'Unknown command'). Cached after first scan, reset by clear_region_cache(), and sourced from the YAML text so it is boot-warm regardless of region-cache state — a player can type 'deeper' on the first attempt after boot. New regions/edge words auto-route on the next boot with no per-direction code edit. MoveCommand._try_wilderness_entry already matched arbitrary direction strings, so the dispatch gate was the entire gap. tests/test_parser_custom_edge_directions.py (9) pins the harvest + the dispatch predicate; 203/203 across wilderness + combat-wilderness + parser-command suites.

design_calls_resolved_recent.date_resolved: 2026-06-12 (design-review batch — confirmed ALREADY resolved in drops 10-11)
design_calls_resolved_recent.decision: Not an open fork. The anticipated 'per-vendor-family grandfather-vs-withdraw' decision never materialized because there were no open shop stock lists to withdraw from — the segmentation violation was the bare `buy` verb FALLTHROUGH (whole registry resolvable by name, anywhere, no vendor gate), not stocked inventory. WeaponData.vendor_stocked (default false) closed the gate FORWARD-ONLY (drops 10-11): the 11 Avail-1 commons are stocked, everything else refuses with a craft/shop redirect, and owned items were never touched (zero inventory rips). Nothing needed grandfathering.

Option b (persist) — Drop 21, surfaced by the new mission_loop smoke. Board mission ids are 'm-'+uuid STRINGS but accept_mission/complete_mission/abandon_mission (db/database.py) did UPDATE ... WHERE id=? against missions.id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, silently affecting 0 rows — so an accepted mission was never persisted and was LOST on server restart (ActiveMissionCommand checks the in-memory board first and falls back to get_active_mission only after restart, where accepted_by had never been written). Within a single session the in-memory board masked it. FIX: the three funcs now match the row by the string id embedded in the JSON data column (WHERE data LIKE the save_mission idiom), and accept upserts (INSERT if the available mission was never written) so accepted_by lands correctly. mission_loop ML2 now asserts get_active_mission(char_id) is non-None after accept (the persistence proof; it returned None pre-fix). 69 mission unit + 4 smoke green.

Option a (localize) — Drop 21, surfaced by the new smuggling_loop smoke. _in_board_room (parser/smuggling_commands.py) read ctx.session.current_room, a PHANTOM attribute never assigned anywhere in server/parser/engine (confirmed sole reader) — so every +smugjobs/smugaccept raised AttributeError, caught by dispatch as 'An error occurred', leaving the smuggling board UNREACHABLE in the live server (undetected because the loop was only ever 'smoke it live, later'). FIX: _in_board_room is now async and reads the room from the DB via ctx.db.get_room(ctx.session.character['room_id']) — the idiom every other room-gated command uses; both call sites (SmugJobsCommand, SmugAcceptCommand) awaited. smuggling_loop now 4 smoke passing (was 1 pass + 3 xfail) + 80 smuggling unit tests green.

Option B delivered (Drop 19). _resolve_equipped_weapon now reads the full ItemInstance (read_equipment) and calls crafted_combat_pips/apply_damage_pips to fold a WEG-faithful, hard-capped pip delta into default_damage and capture accuracy_pips. Numbers: quality band {<=49:-1 pip, 50-69:0 (vendor baseline), 70-89:+1, 90-100:+2}; damage_mod boost = floor(damage_mod/2.0) capped +1; combined damage cap +3 pips (=+1D), floor -1; accuracy floor(accuracy_mod/2.0) capped +1 pip. The forks were ratified: (1) COMBAT-ISOLATION BREACH for equipped crafted gear is sanctioned — the carried-tool/perform_skill_check isolation (Drop F) stays intact; this is a different seam (equipped instance, not carried tool). (2) +1D cap (+3 pips total) chosen. Armor soak = Option C, still FUTURE, blocked on TD.EQUIPMENT_CHARACTER_HOLDS_KEYS_NOT_INSTANCES. Vendor/legacy q50 weapons are unchanged (zero delta). Explicit 'damage <dice>' override paths are intentionally unbuffed (mirrors Drop D guard: damage == default_damage is the equipped-weapon test).

design_calls_resolved_recent.decision: a + vendor flag — open-market buying requires an in-room NPC with ai_config.vendor: true (NOT merely Bargain-skilled: vendors disentangled from hagglers; Lup haggles without becoming an arms dealer unless flagged). Haggle uses the vendor's own Bargain, generic 3D only when the vendor lacks the skill. Curated 8 vendors so every major settlement has a buy point; Kamino deliberately none (commissary world); Gundark teaches, never retails (pinned). Option (b) shop-room flag remains available later as an additive kiosk exception.

Thermal detonator STAYS UNCRAFTABLE even at the black-market tier. Rationale: the X-class icon's scarcity is its identity — vendor/loot-only preserves mystique, and a craftable 10D would dominate the ordnance economy. Drop D's uncraftable pin stands; flip it in a future drop if Brian vetoes.

a — trainer-taught schematics cost TUITION: 50% of the schematic base_cost, minimum 50 cr, charged via adjust_credits(char, -tuition, "schematic_tuition") (a real credit SINK). PC-to-PC teaching stays FREE. Logged caveat honored: tutorial chains ("talk trainer -> craft") must not wall a broke newbie — mechanism decided at implementation (first-lesson-free per trainer recommended). Implementation lands in Gundark Drop G.

a — REMOVE the hard P2P daily cap; KEEP the 5% transaction tax (p2p_tax sink) and the p2p_transfer ledger tag; CONVERT the old threshold into an @economy velocity ALERT (admin-visible, never commerce-blocking). Rationale: segmentation (a) makes PCs the supply chain; a single quality-crafted item legitimately trades above 1,500 cr. Alt-funneling is fought at the faucet throttle + alerts, not by capping legitimate trade. Reverses audit v2 §2.4 with explicit sign-off.

a — adopt availability-tier vendor segmentation: Avail-1 vendor-stocked at book cost AND craftable; Avail-2/3 NOT routinely vendor-stocked (craft/loot/player-shop supply); Avail-4/X = Drop G black-market loop. Implementation gates Lane C vendor placement + Drop G; starts with an existing-shop stock audit and an explicit grandfather-vs-withdraw pass (no silent inventory rips).
